Session: KZero Federation Tracer: DevTools MVP planning thread
Created: 1774892292488
Archived: 1774986699925
Total parts: 5242
==================================================

--- Part 1 ---
You are a senior Chrome extension engineer, TypeScript engineer, and federation troubleshooting specialist.

Build a production-ready Chrome extension called:

KZero Federation Tracer

Primary goal:
Create a Chrome extension that feels like a simpler, more practical, KZero-specific version of SAML Tracer. It must help MSP admins and KZero staff diagnose where an SSO problem is actually coming from during SAML and OIDC setup.

The extension must:
1. capture relevant SAML and OIDC traffic for the active debugging session,
2. decode and inspect the important artifacts,
3. identify likely root causes using deterministic rules first,
4. explain whether the issue is likely on:
   - the KZero / Multi-Pass / Keycloak side,
   - the vendor SaaS / SP side,
   - the network / TLS / WAF / reachability side,
   - the browser / redirect / client-side validation side,
5. map the likely fix back to KZero’s actual custom UI field names instead of only generic Keycloak terminology.

This is not a generic auth viewer. It is a federation diagnostics tool for KZero Passwordless / Multi-Pass / Keycloak-backed tenants.

==================================================
PRODUCT CONTEXT
==================================================

Assume the product context is:

- KZero Passwordless / Multi-Pass is a Keycloak-backed IdP with a custom wrapper UI.
- Realms / tenant names matter and are case sensitive.
- Typical OIDC realm patterns look like:
  - https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>/.well-known/openid-configuration
  - https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>/protocol/openid-connect/auth
  - https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>/protocol/openid-connect/token
  - https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>/protocol/openid-connect/userinfo
  - https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>/protocol/openid-connect/logout
  - https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>/protocol/openid-connect/certs
- Typical SAML realm patterns look like:
  - IdP Entity ID: https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>
  - SSO URL: https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>/protocol/saml
  - SLO URL: https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>/protocol/saml
  - Metadata URL: https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>/protocol/saml/descriptor

The users are usually MSP admins who are not deep identity engineers.
The UX must reduce ambiguity and tell them:
- what happened,
- what was expected,
- what is mismatched,
- where to fix it.

==================================================
AUTHORITATIVE REFERENCES TO USE
==================================================

Use these as the main product references when shaping names, flows, examples, and help text:

1. https://docs.kzero.com/server_admin/index.html#_mpas_features_and_concepts
2. https://desk.kzero.com/portal/en/kb/articles/saml-sso-integration-guide
3. https://desk.kzero.com/portal/en/kb/articles/openid-connect-oidc-sso-integration-guide

Also assume there is an internal/custom troubleshooting prompt that emphasizes:
- start from actual payloads and actual errors,
- distinguish IdP vs SP vs network issues,
- do not invent endpoints,
- prefer OIDC only when the vendor actually supports generic OIDC well,
- map guidance to the custom KZero UI field names when screenshots or custom labels exist,
- do not echo secrets or tokens back unnecessarily.

==================================================
MANDATORY BUILD PRINCIPLES
==================================================

1. Deterministic first
Do not build an AI-only debugger.
The first-class engine must be deterministic:
- parse,
- normalize,
- compare,
- validate,
- run rules,
- generate structured findings.

2. Local-first
Do all capture, decoding, and rules locally in the extension by default.
Do not send captured assertions, tokens, cookies, secrets, or raw traces to any external service.

3. Secure by default
- redact secrets in the UI by default,
- make raw views opt-in,
- never automatically export raw secrets,
- never automatically transmit data externally,
- provide a “sanitized export” option.

4. DevTools-first UX
Make this a DevTools-oriented debugging extension, not just a popup toy.
It should feel appropriate for active federation troubleshooting on the current tab.

5. KZero-specific guidance
Every finding should attempt to answer:
- likely issue location,
- why,
- what exact values are mismatched,
- what KZero field likely needs to be checked,
- what vendor/SP field likely needs to be checked.

==================================================
TECHNICAL DIRECTION
==================================================

Build this as a Chrome Manifest V3 extension with TypeScript.

Preferred architecture:
- DevTools panel for active trace inspection
- background service worker
- optional content scripts only when needed
- chrome.storage.local for local persistence
- a small React UI for the DevTools panel and detail views
- minimal dependencies

Important:
Do not assume basic webRequest alone is enough for the needed diagnostics.
Choose the right capture strategy for:
- redirects,
- query params,
- form POST bodies,
- response bodies when possible,
- page DOM inspection for hidden SAML form posts when relevant.

A good solution may combine:
- DevTools Network visibility,
- debugger-based capture when necessary,
- DOM/content inspection for hidden SAMLResponse / SAMLRequest form fields,
- redirect chain tracking.

You must design around real Chrome extension constraints, not imaginary browser capabilities.

==================================================
SCOPE OF MVP
==================================================

Build a strong MVP first.

The MVP must support:

A. Session capture
- start capture for current tab / inspected tab,
- stop capture,
- clear session,
- show timeline of auth events.

B. Protocol detection
Detect and label:
- SAML Redirect binding
- SAML POST binding
- OIDC authorization requests
- OIDC redirect callback responses
- token requests
- token responses where visible
- discovery fetches
- JWKS fetches
- logout flows

C. Artifact extraction
Extract and normalize:
- URL
- method
- status code
- request params
- response params if visible
- redirect chain
- host / origin
- timing
- relevant headers if useful

For SAML:
- SAMLRequest
- SAMLResponse
- RelayState
- binding type
- XML after decode
- Issuer
- Destination
- Assertion
- Subject
- NameID
- AudienceRestriction
- Conditions
- Recipient
- InResponseTo
- Signature presence
- encryption flags if detectable

For OIDC:
- issuer
- discovery URL
- authorization endpoint
- token endpoint
- userinfo endpoint
- jwks_uri
- client_id
- redirect_uri
- response_type
- response_mode
- scope
- state
- nonce
- code
- error
- error_description
- id_token if present
- access_token only if JWT and safely viewable
- PKCE fields when visible
- token endpoint auth method clues when visible

D. Decoding
- base64 decode where appropriate
- DEFLATE inflate for SAML Redirect requests where needed
- JWT header/payload decode
- XML parsing with robust error handling
- graceful handling of malformed artifacts

E. Findings engine
Produce findings with:
- severity: info / warning / error
- protocol: SAML / OIDC / network / unknown
- likely owner: KZero / vendor SP / network / user data / unknown
- title
- explanation
- evidence
- likely fix
- exact fields to check
- confidence score

==================================================
REQUIRED DIAGNOSTIC RULES
==================================================

Implement these rules at minimum.

SAML rules:
1. Missing SAMLResponse
2. Missing SAMLRequest
3. Unparseable base64 / XML
4. Destination mismatch
5. ACS / Recipient mismatch
6. Audience / Entity ID mismatch
7. Issuer mismatch
8. Missing NameID
9. Likely wrong NameID format
10. Missing or unexpected RelayState behavior
11. Missing assertion signature
12. Missing document signature when relevant
13. Clock skew / NotBefore / NotOnOrAfter issue
14. InResponseTo missing or suspicious
15. IdP-initiated vs SP-initiated mismatch clues
16. Wrong binding clues
17. Certificate / signature validation mismatch clues when detectable
18. Assertion encrypted when SP likely cannot process it
19. AuthnRequest signed expectation mismatch clues
20. ForceAuthn / AllowCreate / NameID policy mismatch clues

OIDC rules:
1. Discovery URL unreachable
2. Discovery issuer mismatch
3. Missing required openid scope
4. redirect_uri mismatch
5. invalid_client
6. invalid_scope
7. unauthorized_client
8. unsupported_response_type
9. unsupported_response_mode
10. state missing or mismatch
11. nonce missing where expected
12. PKCE missing or inconsistent where expected
13. token endpoint auth method mismatch clues
14. JWKS / cert fetch failure
15. issuer mismatch between tokens and discovery
16. login callback contains error / error_description
17. logout redirect mismatch clues
18. public reachability / WAF / TLS suspicion when discovery or JWKS fails
19. callback reached but code/token exchange appears broken
20. access token opaque, not parseable, but not necessarily an error

Cross-protocol / environmental rules:
1. Realm / tenant casing mismatch
2. Wrong host / wrong environment
3. Wrong KZero realm endpoint family
4. Vendor validation likely rejecting otherwise standard metadata
5. Client-side form validation vs backend validation distinction
6. Network/TLS/public accessibility suspicion
7. Suspected stale copied values from another environment
8. Suspected metadata copy/paste truncation

==================================================
KZERO-SPECIFIC FIELD MAPPING
==================================================

The extension must map findings back to KZero’s custom UI labels where possible.

Assume these labels exist in the product and use them in guidance.

OIDC / Identity Provider style labels:
- Redirect URL
- Alias
- Display name
- Display Order
- Client ID
- Client Secret
- Use discovery endpoint
- Discovery Endpoint
- Client authentication
- Client assertion signature algorithm
- Authorization URL
- Token URL
- Logout URL
- User Info URL
- Issuer
- Validate Signatures
- Use PKCE

SAML / Identity Provider style labels:
- Redirect URL
- Alias
- Display name
- Display Order
- Service provider Entity ID
- Use entity descriptor
- SAML entity descriptor
- Identity provider entity ID
- Single Sign-On service url
- Single logout service url
- NameID Policy Format
- Principal type
- Allow create
- HTTP-POST binding response
- HTTP-POST binding for AuthnRequest
- HTTP-POST binding logout
- Want AuthnRequests signed
- Want assertions signed
- Want assertions encrypted
- Force authentication
- Validate signatures
- Sign service provider metadata
- Pass subject
- Allow clock skew
- Attribute Consuming Service Index
- Attribute Consuming Service Name

Application / SAML side labels:
- Client ID
- Name
- Description
- Assertion Consumer Service URL
- NameID Policy Format
- Relay State
- Tenant certificate
- Tenant XML data

When generating suggested fixes, prefer these field labels over generic upstream Keycloak wording.

Examples:
- If OIDC redirect_uri mismatches, tell the user to compare vendor redirect/callback URI against KZero Redirect URL / Valid Redirect URI equivalent.
- If SAML Audience mismatch appears, tell the user to compare the vendor SP Entity ID against KZero Client ID / Service provider Entity ID depending on which screen is being used.
- If SAML ACS mismatch appears, tell the user to compare vendor ACS against Assertion Consumer Service URL.
- If discovery issuer mismatch appears, tell the user to compare Discovery Endpoint and Issuer, and verify tenant casing.

==================================================
UX REQUIREMENTS
==================================================

Design the UI so an MSP can use it under pressure.

The DevTools experience should include:

1. Timeline view
A chronological auth trace:
- discovery
- authorize
- redirects
- callback
- token
- userinfo
- logout
- SAML POST/Redirect events

2. Findings summary
A compact panel showing:
- protocol
- severity
- likely ownership
- one-line diagnosis

3. Finding detail view
Show:
- what was observed
- what was expected
- why it matters
- exact evidence
- likely fix in KZero
- likely fix in vendor SP
- copy-to-ticket summary

4. Artifact inspectors
- decoded SAML XML viewer
- JWT header/payload viewer
- normalized key/value viewer
- raw view behind an explicit toggle

5. Sanitized export
Export a JSON or markdown bundle containing:
- timeline
- normalized artifacts
- findings
- redacted values
- suggested next checks

6. Safe copy helpers
Buttons like:
- Copy sanitized finding
- Copy ticket summary
- Copy vendor support summary

==================================================
PRIVACY AND REDACTION
==================================================

This is critical.

By default:
- redact client secrets
- redact access tokens
- redact refresh tokens
- redact authorization codes unless user reveals them
- partially mask NameID / email / user identifiers
- do not store cookies
- do not transmit captured data

Add a visible notice:
“Captured auth data stays local unless explicitly exported.”

If you add any optional AI summary mode, it must:
- be disabled by default,
- require explicit opt-in,
- only send sanitized bundles,
- never send raw SAML assertions, raw secrets, raw tokens, or cookies.

However:
Do not make AI a required part of the MVP.

==================================================
IMPLEMENTATION REQUIREMENTS
==================================================

Use:
- TypeScript
- clean modular architecture
- strong typing for protocol events and findings
- reusable parsers
- test fixtures
- unit tests for decoding and rules
- sample traces for happy path and common failures

Suggested modules:
- capture/
- parsers/
- normalizers/
- rules/
- mappings/
- ui/
- export/
- fixtures/
- tests/

Define strong internal models for:
- RawCaptureEvent
- NormalizedSamlEvent
- NormalizedOidcEvent
- Finding
- SuggestedFix
- SanitizedExportBundle

==================================================
WHAT TO BUILD FIRST
==================================================

Do not start by over-polishing UI.

Build in this order:

Phase 1:
- architecture
- manifest
- session capture scaffold
- event normalization
- SAML decode path
- OIDC decode path
- findings engine skeleton
- basic DevTools UI

Phase 2:
- KZero field mapping
- richer finding cards
- export
- test fixtures
- better filtering

Phase 3:
- polish
- better visuals
- optional side panel
- optional AI summary mode
- docs

==================================================
DELIVERABLES YOU MUST PRODUCE
==================================================

Produce a real implementation, not high-level advice only.

I want:
1. repo structure
2. manifest and extension wiring
3. TypeScript source files
4. parsing utilities
5. rules engine
6. initial React UI
7. fixtures
8. tests
9. README with install/run/debug instructions
10. a short architecture note explaining Chrome API choices and tradeoffs

==================================================
IMPORTANT ENGINEERING CONSTRAINTS
==================================================

- Do not invent unsupported browser APIs.
- Do not assume unrestricted access to response bodies without choosing a valid capture method.
- Do not build a cloud backend in v1.
- Do not require a database server.
- Do not use heavyweight frameworks unnecessarily.
- Do not hide all logic behind LLM calls.
- Do not hardcode customer-specific secrets.
- Do not log raw secrets to console.
- Do not create fake “working” code with placeholders everywhere.

==================================================
SUCCESS CRITERIA
==================================================

The MVP is successful if a tester can:
1. open DevTools,
2. start a capture,
3. run through an OIDC or SAML login,
4. see the relevant events,
5. inspect decoded artifacts,
6. get a useful diagnosis like:
   - “Redirect URI mismatch: likely vendor SP config issue”
   - “SAML Audience mismatch: likely SP Entity ID mismatch”
   - “Discovery issuer mismatch: likely wrong tenant/realm or case mismatch”
   - “JWT/JWKS validation issue: likely issuer/JWKS/reachability problem”
   - “SAML assertion expired: likely clock skew or stale session”
7. export a sanitized summary.

==================================================
OUTPUT FORMAT FOR YOUR RESPONSE
==================================================

Respond in this order:
1. short implementation plan
2. architecture decisions
3. repo tree
4. code files
5. tests
6. README
7. next risks / limitations

Ask clarifying questions only if absolutely blocked.
Otherwise make sensible implementation decisions and start building.

Now build the extension.

--- Part 195 ---
1) **Short implementation plan**
- Build a real MV3 extension with a DevTools-first workflow: DevTools network capture + background session engine + React panel UI.
- Add deterministic pipeline: raw capture -> normalize -> decode (SAML/OIDC/JWT) -> rule engine -> KZero field-mapped findings.
- Enforce local-first + redaction defaults + sanitized export.
- Add fixtures and unit tests for parsers/rules.
- Ship docs for install/debug and architecture tradeoffs.

2) **Architecture decisions**
- Capture strategy:
  - `chrome.devtools.network.onRequestFinished` in `src/devtools/index.ts` for inspected-tab request/response metadata and response body (`getContent`).
  - `chrome.webRequest.onErrorOccurred` in `src/background/index.ts` for network/TLS/WAF-style failures.
  - `content_scripts` form submit interception in `src/content/index.ts` for hidden SAML POST artifacts.
- Processing:
  - Session store in `src/capture/sessionStore.ts`.
  - Normalizers in `src/normalizers/index.ts`.
  - Decoders/parsers in `src/parsers/saml.ts`, `src/parsers/oidc.ts`, `src/parsers/jwt.ts`.
  - Deterministic rules in `src/rules/*` with KZero field mapping in `src/mappings/fieldMappings.ts`.
- UI:
  - React DevTools panel in `src/panel/App.tsx` with timeline, findings, detail, artifact inspector, safe copy buttons, and sanitized export.
- Security/privacy:
  - Redaction defaults via `src/shared/redaction.ts`.
  - Export bundle is local + sanitized via `src/export/sanitizedExport.ts`.
  - No external transmission path included in MVP.

3) **Repo tree**
```text
.
├─ ARCHITECTURE.md
├─ README.md
├─ package.json
├─ tsconfig.json
├─ scripts/
│  ├─ build.mjs
│  └─ clean.mjs
├─ src/
│  ├─ background/
│  │  └─ index.ts
│  ├─ capture/
│  │  └─ sessionStore.ts
│  ├─ content/
│  │  └─ index.ts
│  ├─ devtools/
│  │  └─ index.ts
│  ├─ export/
│  │  └─ sanitizedExport.ts
│  ├─ fixtures/
│  │  ├─ oidc-redirect-mismatch.json
│  │  └─ saml-audience-mismatch.json
│  ├─ mappings/
│  │  └─ fieldMappings.ts
│  ├─ normalizers/
│  │  └─ index.ts
│  ├─ panel/
│  │  ├─ App.tsx
│  │  ├─ main.tsx
│  │  └─ styles.css
│  ├─ parsers/
│  │  ├─ jwt.ts
│  │  ├─ oidc.ts
│  │  └─ saml.ts
│  ├─ rules/
│  │  ├─ crossRules.ts
│  │  ├─ helpers.ts
│  │  ├─ index.ts
│  │  ├─ oidcRules.ts
│  │  └─ samlRules.ts
│  ├─ shared/
│  │  ├─ messages.ts
│  │  ├─ models.ts
│  │  ├─ redaction.ts
│  │  └─ utils.ts
│  └─ static/
│     ├─ devtools.html
│     ├─ manifest.json
│     └─ panel.html
└─ tests/
   ├─ jwt.test.ts
   ├─ rules.test.ts
   └─ saml.test.ts
```

4) **Code files**
- Extension wiring:
  - `src/static/manifest.json` defines MV3 service worker, devtools page, content script, permissions, host access.
  - `src/static/devtools.html` and `src/static/panel.html` are entry pages.
- Capture + session:
  - `src/devtools/index.ts` serializes DevTools requests into `RawCaptureEvent`.
  - `src/background/index.ts` manages sessions, runtime messaging, panel updates, and webRequest error capture.
  - `src/capture/sessionStore.ts` stores raw/normalized/findings per tab and persists to `chrome.storage.local`.
- Protocol extraction/decoding:
  - `src/normalizers/index.ts` detects/labels SAML/OIDC/discovery/callback/token/jwks/logout and extracts key artifacts.
  - `src/parsers/saml.ts` handles base64 + redirect DEFLATE + XML parse + SAML fields.
  - `src/parsers/jwt.ts` decodes JWT header/payload.
  - `src/parsers/oidc.ts` parses callback/query/fragment and token response artifacts.
- Findings engine:
  - `src/rules/samlRules.ts`, `src/rules/oidcRules.ts`, `src/rules/crossRules.ts` implement deterministic diagnostics with severity/owner/confidence/evidence.
  - `src/mappings/fieldMappings.ts` maps findings to KZero custom field labels and vendor-side fields.
- UI:
  - `src/panel/App.tsx` provides:
    - capture controls,
    - timeline,
    - findings summary/detail,
    - artifact inspector with raw toggle,
    - copy helpers,
    - sanitized export download.
  - `src/panel/styles.css` provides responsive DevTools-oriented layout.
- Export/redaction:
  - `src/shared/redaction.ts` masks secrets/identifiers by default.
  - `src/export/sanitizedExport.ts` creates local sanitized JSON bundle.

5) **Tests**
- Added fixtures:
  - `src/fixtures/oidc-redirect-mismatch.json`
  - `src/fixtures/saml-audience-mismatch.json`
- Unit tests:
  - `tests/jwt.test.ts`: JWT decode.
  - `tests/saml.test.ts`: SAML POST decode + redirect DEFLATE decode.
  - `tests/rules.test.ts`: finding generation for redirect URI mismatch and SAML audience mismatch.
- Validation run:
  - `npm run build` succeeded.
  - `npm test` succeeded (5/5 tests passing).

6) **README**
- `README.md` includes:
  - product purpose and MVP scope,
  - privacy defaults,
  - repo structure,
  - install/build/load-unpacked steps,
  - DevTools usage flow,
  - test commands,
  - rule coverage summary,
  - Chrome API constraints/tradeoffs reference (`ARCHITECTURE.md`).

7) **Next risks / limitations**
- DevTools response body visibility is best-effort; some responses may not expose content.
- Some “clue” rules are heuristic where protocol context is incomplete (still deterministic, but lower confidence).
- Signature/cert validation is inferred from observable artifacts, not full crypto validation in MVP.
- No full HAR import/replay yet; current flow is live-tab trace only.
- If you want next, I can add:
  1. explicit rule-id catalog UI filter and per-rule docs,
  2. richer SAML XML tree inspector with XPath highlights,
  3. optional side panel and persisted multi-session history.

--- Part 197 ---
Add all three

--- Part 283 ---
Implemented — all three are now added and working.

- Added rule ID catalog filtering + per-rule docs in the panel UI (`src/panel/App.tsx`, `src/shared/ruleCatalog.ts`).
- Added a richer SAML XML inspector with XPath-style rows and highlighted important claims/conditions (`src/panel/App.tsx`, `src/panel/styles.css`).
- Added optional Chrome Side Panel plus persisted multi-session history (`src/static/manifest.json`, `src/static/sidepanel.html`, `src/sidepanel/main.tsx`, `src/capture/sessionStore.ts`, `src/background/index.ts`, `scripts/build.mjs`).

Key details of what changed:

- **Rule catalog + filter**
  - Rule filter dropdown now filters findings by explicit `ruleId`.
  - Added per-rule documentation panel (summary, why it matters, KZero checks, vendor checks).
  - Added “Copy rule ID” helper.
  - Files: `src/panel/App.tsx`, `src/shared/ruleCatalog.ts`.

- **SAML XPath inspector**
  - Decoded SAML XML is parsed via `DOMParser`.
  - Inspector renders XPath-like paths (`/Response/Assertion/...`) including attributes (`/@Destination`, etc.).
  - Highlights critical nodes like `Issuer`, `Audience`, `NameID`, `Recipient`, `InResponseTo`, `NotBefore`, `NotOnOrAfter`.
  - Respects redaction when raw toggle is off.
  - Files: `src/panel/App.tsx`, `src/panel/styles.css`.

- **Side panel + persisted history**
  - MV3 side panel configured and enabled on action click.
  - Added side panel entry page and React mount.
  - Session snapshots are now persisted to `chrome.storage.local` on stop capture (bounded history).
  - Added `GET_HISTORY` + `LOAD_HISTORY_ITEM` messaging and history UI section to reload past traces.
  - Files: `src/static/manifest.json`, `src/static/sidepanel.html`, `src/sidepanel/main.tsx`, `src/capture/sessionStore.ts`, `src/shared/messages.ts`, `src/shared/models.ts`, `src/background/index.ts`, `scripts/build.mjs`.

- **Docs updated**
  - README and architecture note updated for side panel/history/rule catalog/XPath inspector.
  - Files: `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`.

Validation completed:

- `npm run build` passed.
- `npm test` passed (5 tests).
- Dist output includes side panel artifacts: `dist/sidepanel.html`, `dist/sidepanel.js`.

Next step: reload the unpacked extension in `chrome://extensions` so the new side panel and manifest changes take effect.

--- Part 285 ---
the interface is very clunky and does not look polished. what do you recommend? I also want to see more details regarding how to fix the error instead of just telling me what the error is. how should we do that? think about it. 

--- Part 287 ---
**Planning UI Recommendations**

The user is looking for recommendations rather than code changes, so I need to provide a thoughtful plan. I’ll suggest a focus on UI polish and richer guidance. I'll mention extending DevTools with a clear strategy involving information architecture, layout, typography, and interactions. It’s essential to maintain a purposeful design within existing structures. Concrete UX improvements could include a split view, minimizing clutter, and using a searchable timeline. I'll propose a two-step design: "Diagnosis" and "Evidence" views for better user experience.

--- Part 288 ---
**Designing a Fix Wizard**

I’m envisioning a "Fix Wizard" that provides dynamic steps for each rule. This includes a "Compare values" table linked to KZero field mapping and "copy value" buttons, all while highlighting expected versus observed differences. I'll add indicators for confidence and ownership. Structured steps for each rule will explain what to check in KZero, including UI paths and comparisons. A decision tree will help confirm mismatches, and quick confirmation checks will be included. Additionally, I'll propose suggesting documentation references and embedding relevant Markdown without web fetching.

--- Part 289 ---
**Enhancing SAML Audience Handling**

I’m planning to address SAML audience mismatches by comparing the vendor SP entity from the request to the KZero service provider entity ID, but I must ensure that no sensitive information like raw tokens is displayed. I want to suggest a better layout with a left column for a timeline, a central area for findings, and a right side for details with tabs. Adding a search bar, filters, and collapsible panels will enhance usability. I’ll need to reference KZero UI navigation flows and consider embedding help links to KZero docs for added support.

--- Part 290 ---
**Proposing Local-First Changes**

The user mentioned a local-first approach, which allows linking without sending data, so I want to suggest a modeling structure for each rule. This would include a SuggestedFix object with paths and steps. I also propose a "Fix preview" mode to suggest changes without auto-filling. 

I’ll call out current interface issues like excessive JSON and poor grid design. Adopting a design system that aligns with DevTools UI could help, using CSS variables for themes. The plan includes steps for redesigning information architecture, enhancing visual appeal, and developing a fix recipes engine.

--- Part 291 ---
**Creating an Actionable Plan**

I’m proposing actionable steps starting with a "Fix checklist" that includes toggles and questions for vendor support. I’ll structure it into sections: "KZero changes," "Vendor changes," "Network checks," and "Verify," each with bullet steps. Storing extracted values in evidence with keys and allowing copying is a must. 

For the UX, I’ll suggest restructuring the UI into three panels with tabs, improving layout, and displaying key fields without clutter. The fix detail will involve implementing "FixRecipe" functions for step generation and including context diffs for string mismatches. Additionally, I’ll unify side panels and DevTools for a smoother experience.

--- Part 292 ---
The clunkiness is coming from two things: (1) the UI is basically “lists + raw JSON,” and (2) the “Finding detail” is descriptive, not procedural. I’d fix both by treating this like a diagnostics console with guided remediation, not a network viewer.

What I recommend

**1) Redesign the information architecture (make it feel like a tool, not a dump)**
- Switch to a stable 3-pane layout with tabs in the detail pane:
  - Left: Timeline (with protocol chips + search)
  - Middle: Findings (grouped by “Most likely root cause” + severity)
  - Right (detail): tabs: `Fix Steps` (default), `What Happened`, `Evidence`, `Artifacts`
- Stop showing “artifacts JSON” as the primary inspector. Replace it with a “Key fields” table per event (SAML: Issuer/Destination/Recipient/Audience/NameID/NotOnOrAfter; OIDC: issuer/client_id/redirect_uri/scope/state/error).
- Use progressive disclosure: show 6–10 fields, then “Show more,” then raw JSON behind the sensitive toggle.

**2) Make findings actionable with “Fix Recipes”**
Right now a Finding is basically: title/explanation/observed/expected. For MSP admins, the missing piece is an ordered checklist that answers: “Where do I click in KZero, what do I compare, what exact value should it be, and how do I verify it worked?”

Implement a deterministic “Fix Recipe” layer:
- Add a `FixRecipe` generator per rule ID (not AI):
  - Inputs: finding + normalized events (and any extracted values like realm, issuer, redirect_uri, SAML Destination/Recipient)
  - Output: structured steps with exact KZero field labels and vendor field labels
- Render that recipe in the `Fix Steps` tab with copy buttons for the *expected* values (sanitized by default).

Concretely, each recipe should produce sections like:
- `KZero (Multi-Pass) checks` (step-by-step, using your custom labels)
- `Vendor/SP checks`
- `Network/TLS checks` (only when relevant)
- `Verify` (what to re-run and what success looks like in the trace)
- `If still failing` (next deterministic checks, what evidence to attach to a ticket)

**Example (OIDC_REDIRECT_URI_MISMATCH) – what “better” looks like**
Instead of only stating mismatch, the recipe would show:
- KZero:
  - Check `Redirect URL` equals: `https://vendor.example.com/callback` (exact)
  - Confirm `Use discovery endpoint` + `Discovery Endpoint` points to the correct tenant realm (case-sensitive)
- Vendor:
  - Update “Redirect URI / Callback URL” to the exact value above (scheme/host/path/trailing slash)
  - Confirm client type (public/confidential) matches KZero `Client authentication`
- Verify:
  - Re-run login; ensure authorize request `redirect_uri` and callback URL match exactly; no `error` in callback
- Copy helpers:
  - “Copy expected redirect URI”
  - “Copy vendor support summary” (no code/tokens)

**3) Visual polish (without heavy deps)**
- Adopt a DevTools-like visual language: compact density, clear typographic hierarchy, consistent chips/badges.
- Replace the current “big grid + long lists” with:
  - sticky section headers
  - severity badges (`Error`, `Warning`, `Info`) + owner badge (`KZero`, `Vendor`, `Network`, `Browser`)
  - a “Top diagnosis” card at the top (one-liner + confidence + owner)
- Add lightweight affordances:
  - diff highlighting for observed vs expected (character-level or segment-level)
  - hover-to-reveal truncated values
  - keyboard navigation (up/down selects finding; enter toggles details)
- Keep motion minimal but intentional: selection transitions + reveal of “Fix Steps” sections.

How we should implement this in the codebase (deterministic, maintainable)
- Add `src/rules/recipes/`:
  - `types.ts` (`FixRecipe`, `FixStep`, `VerifyStep`, `CopySnippet`)
  - `index.ts` maps `ruleId -> recipeBuilder(finding, context)`
- Extend the Finding pipeline (not the capture) so it stays local-first:
  - Build a “context” object from normalized events (realm, kzeroHost/env family, issuer candidates, SAML endpoints, etc.)
- Update UI:
  - Replace the current detail body with a `FindingDetail` component that renders:
    - “Diagnosis” (1–2 lines)
    - “Fix Steps” (recipe)
    - “Evidence” (auto-curated, not raw)
    - “Artifacts” (key fields table + optional raw)

If you want, I can implement this as the next iteration by:
1) introducing `FixRecipe` + a few high-impact recipe implementations first (redirect_uri mismatch, discovery issuer mismatch, SAML audience/ACS mismatch, clock skew),
2) refactoring the panel into the 3-pane + detail tabs layout,
3) replacing JSON-first inspection with “key fields” tables + diff highlighting.

--- Part 364 ---
**Clarifying loadHistory and useEffect**

I noticed an issue with the `loadHistory` function being defined after it’s called in the `useEffect` on line 256. But actually, the effect callback isn’t executed until after the render, so `loadHistory` will indeed be assigned by that time, which is fine. However, there’s the TypeScript/ES semantics: during the render, the `useEffect` registers the callback that captures `loadHistory`, putting it in the temporal dead zone (TDZ) until its declaration. But is it accessed then? It’s not executed, just referenced, which seems to be allowed.

--- Part 450 ---
Added “product UI” troubleshooting help directly into the extension.

What you get now

- In **Fix steps**, each “Fix in KZero” section now includes a **Check fields on the current page** table:
  - Click **Check fields** and the extension will scan the *actual page you have open in the inspected tab* (KZero Passwordless UI or Keycloak admin console) for the relevant field labels.
  - It shows **Current** vs **Expected** (when we have a deterministic expected value from the finding/recipe).
  - Click **Locate** to auto-scroll + highlight the matching field on the page.
  - Secrets (Client Secret / tokens) are **masked unless Raw values is enabled**.

Files that implement this
- UI + table + locate buttons: `src/panel/App.tsx`, `src/panel/styles.css`
- Deterministic fix “recipes” + expected values wiring: `src/recipes/buildRecipe.ts`
- Page scanner/highlighter (content script): `src/content/index.ts`
- Background routing (panel <-> content): `src/background/index.ts`
- Label aliases (KZero label vs Keycloak label differences): `src/mappings/uiLabelAliases.ts`

About the error screenshot (duplicate React keys)
- That warning was from the old XPath renderer keying rows by `path+value`. It’s fixed, but **Chrome will keep showing old extension errors until you reload the unpacked extension**.
- Run `npm run build`, then go to `chrome://extensions` and click **Reload** on the unpacked extension (or remove + re-load `dist/`).

How to use it with your KZero screenshots
- Open DevTools on the **same tab** where you’re viewing the KZero Integration screen (e.g., SAML app config / OIDC app config) or the Keycloak console screen.
- Select a finding → **Fix steps** → **Check fields**.

--- Part 642 ---
Two separate issues here:

1) Side Panel width “taking over” the page
- This is a Chrome Side Panel constraint: the panel is browser UI that *always consumes horizontal space*. The webpage only gets the remaining viewport. If the site (Zoho, etc.) doesn’t respond well, logos/menus disappear at narrower widths.
- We can’t force third‑party sites to keep their layout visible.

What we can do (and I implemented the right approach earlier):
- Use **Pop out** (opens the tracer in a separate window) when you need more space without shrinking the page.
- Keep the Side Panel narrow; the tracer now switches to a single-pane tab layout at small widths (Findings/Detail/Timeline/History) so it stays usable without being wide.

2) “Multi-Pass” / “Keycloak” must never be mentioned
Fixed. I removed all visible references and renamed the one rule title.

Changes made:
- UI header + tips now say “KZero Passwordless” only: `src/panel/App.tsx`
- README no longer mentions Multi-Pass/Keycloak: `README.md`
- Rule title updated: `src/rules/crossRules.ts`
- Verified there are no remaining mentions in the repo.

## Goal

Build and deliver **KZero Federation Tracer**, a production-ready Chrome MV3 extension for KZero Passwordless MSP admins to diagnose SAML and OIDC SSO problems. The extension must capture federation traffic, decode artifacts, run deterministic diagnostic rules, map findings to KZero UI field labels, and provide guided fix steps.

## Instructions

- Build in TypeScript with a clean modular architecture (no heavy frameworks beyond React)
- Use Manifest V3, service worker, DevTools panel, Side Panel, and content scripts
- Never mention "Keycloak" or "Multi-Pass" — always "KZero Passwordless"
- Redact secrets by default; local-first; no external transmission
- Deterministic rules engine first (not AI); deterministic Fix Recipes per rule ID
- KZero brand color is `#f85c3a` (orange); extension icons generated from SVG at build time
- Extension must work via Side Panel ("Use current tab" targeting) and via popup window ("Pop out")

## Discoveries

- The original UI was a clunky "lists + raw JSON" layout; it was rebuilt as a 3-pane diagnostics console with tabs and a "Fix Steps" default detail view
- A deterministic **Fix Recipe** layer was added — each finding generates procedural steps mapped to KZero field labels, expected values, vendor checks, and verification steps
- **Product UI field scanning** was implemented: content script scans visible form fields on the target tab (using label matching + alias support) and the panel shows Current vs Expected in a table; "Locate" scroll-highlights fields
- Side Panel width issue: Chrome Side Panel always consumes horizontal space from the webpage — this is a browser-level constraint. The extension now switches to single-pane tab layout at narrow widths and has a "Pop out" button to open in a separate window
- The `isPopup` / `isNarrow` / `narrowTab` / `openPopup` state variables were referenced in the narrow-layout JSX but their `useState` declarations were missing — caused runtime `ReferenceError` — fixed
- Brand color `#f85c3a` applied to extension icon (SVG → PNG at build via `@resvg/resvg-js`), primary button, LIVE badge, and gradient accent line
- All "Multi-Pass" and "Keycloak" strings were removed from UI copy, rule titles, and README
- **Zoho One test**: after clicking "Use current tab" in Side Panel, no federation events were captured during a login flow — this is the current unresolved issue

## Accomplished

### Core extension (fully built)
- Project scaffold: `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `scripts/build.mjs`, `scripts/clean.mjs`, `scripts/generate-icons.mjs`
- MV3 manifest, service worker, DevTools entry, content scripts
- `src/capture/sessionStore.ts` — per-tab session store with `chrome.storage.local` persistence
- `src/normalizers/index.ts` — raw event → NormalizedEvent (SAML/OIDC/network detection + field extraction)
- `src/parsers/saml.ts` — base64 + DEFLATE decode + XML parse with fast-xml-parser + claim extraction
- `src/parsers/jwt.ts` — JWT header/payload decode
- `src/parsers/oidc.ts` — OIDC callback/token artifact extraction
- `src/rules/samlRules.ts` — ~18 deterministic SAML checks (missing request/response, decode failures, destination/ACS/audience/issuer mismatch, missing NameID, clock skew, relay state, signature flags, binding clues, encrypted assertion, ForceAuthn policy)
- `src/rules/oidcRules.ts` — ~18 deterministic OIDC checks (discovery unreachable, issuer mismatch, missing openid scope, redirect_uri mismatch, protocol errors, state mismatch, nonce missing, PKCE inconsistency, JWKS failure, token/discovery issuer mismatch, callback token exchange break, opaque access token, auth method mismatch, logout redirect)
- `src/rules/crossRules.ts` — cross-cutting checks (realm case mismatch, wrong endpoint family, wrong host/environment, vendor rejecting metadata, client vs backend validation distinction, network/TLS suspicion, stale copied values)
- `src/mappings/fieldMappings.ts` — KZero field label → vendor field label mapping per rule
- `src/mappings/uiLabelAliases.ts` — label alias expansion for UI field scanning (e.g., "Redirect URL" → "Valid Redirect URIs")
- `src/rules/index.ts` — findings engine with deduplication
- `src/export/sanitizedExport.ts` — sanitized JSON export bundle
- `src/shared/redaction.ts` — secret/identifier masking
- `src/shared/ruleCatalog.ts` — per-rule documentation (short description, why it matters, KZero checks, vendor checks)
- `src/recipes/types.ts`, `src/recipes/context.ts`, `src/recipes/buildRecipe.ts` — Fix Recipe system with 12+ rule-specific implementations
- `src/panel/KZeroLogo.tsx` — embedded KZero wordmark SVG from kzero.com
- `src/panel/App.tsx` — complete React panel with 3-pane + narrow single-pane tab layout, timeline, findings, detail tabs (Fix Steps / What Happened / Evidence / Artifacts / SAML XML), DiffLine component, Key Fields table, XPath inspector, Check fields table, rule ID filter, text search
- `src/panel/styles.css` — KZero-branded dark theme with `#f85c3a` orange accents
- `src/static/icon.svg` — branded extension icon (orange background, white KZero mark)
- `src/fixtures/oidc-redirect-mismatch.json`, `src/fixtures/saml-audience-mismatch.json`
- `tests/jwt.test.ts`, `tests/saml.test.ts`, `tests/rules.test.ts` — all 5 tests passing
- `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`

### Features added (subsequent rounds)
- Rule ID catalog filter + per-rule docs in panel
- SAML XML XPath inspector with highlights for important nodes
- Side Panel support with session history persistence
- **Fix Recipes** with KZero field expectations, copy helpers, verify checklists
- Product UI field scanner (content script → background → panel) with "Check fields" and "Locate" buttons
- UI field label alias support
- Side Panel "Pop out" to separate popup window (via `chrome.windows.create`)
- Narrow-width single-pane tab layout for the panel
- Extension icons generated from SVG at build time
- `#f85c3a` orange branding throughout

### Not yet working / in progress
- **Capture not triggering** in Side Panel "Use current tab" mode: clicking "Use current tab" after navigating a Zoho One login flow resulted in zero captured events. The likely cause is that `chrome.sidePanel` only captures events in the **DevTools-inspected tab**, not arbitrary tabs via Side Panel. The current architecture wires DevTools capture to the inspected tab only. The Side Panel has "Check fields" targeting but **does not currently have active network capture for the target tab** — it only reads history. The background `webRequest.onErrorOccurred` fires globally, but `chrome.devtools.network.onRequestFinished` only fires for the DevTools-inspected tab.

## Next steps (priority order)

1. **Fix the Side Panel capture gap** — the most critical open issue. The Side Panel's "Use current tab" correctly targets a tab for field scanning, but does **not** capture network events for that tab. The architecture needs one of:
   - **Option A**: Add `chrome.webRequest` (not just `onErrorOccurred`) to capture all requests globally and route them to the active session. Requires `webRequest` permission and careful filtering to avoid noise.
   - **Option B**: Detect when the Side Panel is open and the inspected tab IS the target tab — prompt the user to use DevTools for capture; Side Panel is review-only.
   - **Option C**: Use `chrome.debugger` API (requires user permission to attach) to capture network from arbitrary tabs.
   - Recommended: start with **Option A** (global webRequest listener filtering by active session's tabId) — lowest friction, no extra permissions beyond what's already declared.

2. **After fixing capture**: verify Zoho OIDC flow produces findings (redirect_uri mismatch is the most likely for a new SP integration).

3. Add more Fix Recipes for remaining rule IDs that currently fall through to the default block.

4. Consider a "KZero Passwordless navigation hint" per finding (e.g., which admin console screen to open, which section the field lives in).

--- Part 669 ---
Now I have a clear picture. The problem is:

1. `chrome.webRequest.onErrorOccurred` only fires on errors - not on successful responses
2. The normal login flow (Zoho OIDC) won't trigger errors, so nothing is captured
3. There's no `webRequest.onCompleted` listener to capture successful requests

The fix is to add `chrome.webRequest.onCompleted` which fires for all completed requests (success or error). But I need to be careful:
- This could capture a lot of noise
- I should only capture requests for active sessions
- The `webRequest` permission is already declared, but I need `webRequest` not just `webRequest.onErrorOccurred` in the permissions


- I also need to update the manifest to include the full `webRequest` permission so the `onCompleted` event actually works
- Response bodies won't be available through `webRequest` in Manifest V3, so I'll need to rely on request metadata like URL, method, status code, headers, and query parameters
- The `onHeadersReceived` event won't help since `responseBody` access requires the `webRequest` permission which won't be granted, so I'll stick with `onCompleted` for request/response metadata without the body content
- Since the background script already runs with host permissions for all URLs through the manifest, I can filter the captured requests by checking if a session is active and matching the tabId, so I only process federation-related traffic
- There's a potential race condition where the session might not be active when a webRequest fires, but I'll let the session store handle that check
- Now I'm updating the manifest permissions to include the full `webRequest` permission, and I need to handle the fact that the response body won't be accessible in Manifest V3 anyway due to MV3 restrictions, so I'll focus on capturing request headers, URL, status code, and other metadata instead

--- Part 723 ---
There were two separate bugs causing zero capture in Side Panel mode. Both are fixed now.

**Bug 1: `webRequest.onCompleted` was missing**
The background only had `webRequest.onErrorOccurred` — which only fires on failures. Normal successful auth requests (200s, 302s, 301s) were silently ignored. Added `chrome.webRequest.onCompleted` to capture **all** completed requests globally.

**Bug 2: "Start" routed to wrong tab**
In Side Panel mode, `getTabId("sidepanel")` returns `-1`, so "Start" was sending `START_CAPTURE` for tabId `-1` while webRequest events came in with the real tab id — they never matched. Fixed: "Start" now sends the `effectiveTabId` (the actual target tab from "Use current tab").

Files changed:
- `src/background/index.ts` — added `webRequest.onCompleted` listener (captures all completed requests globally)
- `src/panel/App.tsx` — "Start" now uses `effectiveTabId` so capture targets the right tab

## Goal

Build **KZero Federation Tracer**, a production-ready Chrome MV3 extension for KZero Passwordless MSP admins to diagnose SAML and OIDC SSO problems. The extension captures federation traffic, decodes artifacts, runs deterministic diagnostic rules, maps findings to KZero UI field labels, and provides guided fix steps.

## Instructions

- TypeScript, clean modular architecture, no heavy frameworks beyond React
- MV3: service worker, DevTools panel, Side Panel, popup window, content scripts
- KZero brand color is `#f85c3a` (orange); extension icons generated from SVG at build time via `@resvg/resvg-js`
- Redact secrets by default; local-first; no external transmission
- Deterministic rules engine first (not AI); deterministic Fix Recipes per rule ID mapped to KZero field labels
- Never mention "Keycloak" or "Multi-Pass" — always "KZero Passwordless"
- Extension must work via Side Panel ("Use current tab" targeting) and via popup window ("Pop out")
- WebRequest capture must work globally (not just DevTools-inspected tab)

## Discoveries

- The original UI was a clunky "lists + raw JSON" layout; rebuilt as a 3-pane diagnostics console with tabs and "Fix Steps" as default detail view
- Deterministic **Fix Recipe** layer added — each finding generates procedural steps: KZero field labels, expected values, vendor checks, verification steps, copy helpers
- **Product UI field scanning** implemented: content script scans visible form fields on the target tab using label matching + alias expansion; panel shows Current vs Expected table; "Locate" scroll-highlights fields
- Side Panel width is a Chrome-level constraint — the panel always consumes horizontal space from the webpage. Extension now has narrow single-pane tab layout + "Pop out" button for separate window
- `isPopup` / `isNarrow` / `narrowTab` / `openPopup` state variables were referenced in narrow-layout JSX but their `useState` declarations were missing — caused `ReferenceError` in Side Panel — fixed
- `#f85c3a` orange branding applied to extension icon (SVG → PNG at build), primary button, LIVE badge, gradient accent line
- All "Multi-Pass" and "Keycloak" strings removed from UI copy, rule titles, README
- **Critical capture bug**: `webRequest.onCompleted` was missing from background — only `onErrorOccurred` was present, so successful auth requests (200s, 302s) were silently ignored
- **Critical routing bug**: Side Panel "Start" sent `START_CAPTURE` for `tabId = -1` while webRequest events came in with the real target tab id — sessions never matched. Fixed by routing "Start" through `effectiveTabId`

- `src/parsers/oidc.ts` — OIDC callback/token artifact extraction

### Features added across rounds
- Rule ID catalog filter + per-rule docs
- SAML XML XPath inspector with highlights
- Side Panel support with session history persistence
- Fix Recipes with KZero field expectations + verify checklists

- DevTools panel "Start" is unaffected but should be verified

--- Part 977 ---
The `"fetch"` type is not valid for webRequest. `xmlhttprequest` already covers XHR, and `fetch` requests come through as `other` or aren't captured by webRequest at all in MV3. The error is that `"fetch"` is simply not a valid type in the list.

--- Part 982 ---
This is a benign error that happens when Chrome moves the panel page into the back/forward cache (bfcache). It's not a real error - it just means the page was suspended and the message channel was closed. This happens commonly when navigating between pages. It's not something we can prevent - it's a Chrome internal behavior. It's just a warning being logged by Chrome.

The "Context: Unknown" with the anonymous function at line 0 is typical of these bfcache warnings. These are harmless - the extension continues to work fine after the page is restored from bfcache.

I should tell the user this is benign and doesn't affect functionality.

--- Part 983 ---
That's benign — Chrome moved the panel page into its back/forward cache when you navigated away (or it went idle), which closes the message port. The extension keeps working fine once the panel is active again. You can ignore it, or if it's noisy in the console, we can add a cleanup handler.

Actually, the simplest approach is to make `ensureSession` restore from storage first:

```typescript
const ensureSession = async (tabId: number): Promise<CaptureSession> => {
  const existing = sessions.get(tabId);
  if (existing) return existing;
  
  const stored = await storageGet<CaptureSession>(`session:${tabId}`);
  if (stored) {
    sessions.set(tabId, stored);
    return stored;
  }
  
  const created: CaptureSession = {
    tabId,
    active: false,
    rawEvents: [],
    normalizedEvents: [],
    findings: []
  };
  sessions.set(tabId, created);
  return created;
};
```

The issue is likely that the content script isn't properly registered or the message isn't reaching it. The content script listens on a port and handles scan requests, but I need to verify the message routing is actually working end-to-end.

Looking at how the background manages content connections, it only opens ports when it receives messages from content scripts. So the background has a port to content, but when the panel sends a UI scan request, the background needs to route it correctly to the right tab's content script.

The flow should be: panel sends the scan request, background receives it and forwards to the target tab's content script, which scans and sends results back through the port. I see the problem. When `REQUEST_UI_SCAN` is received:
1. It looks for `contentPorts.get(message.tabId)` — but this is the **panel's messaging tab ID**, not the target tab ID
2. If there's no content port for that tab, it returns "No content script connected" error

But the panel sends the request using `effectiveTabId` (the target tab). The issue is that `contentPorts` maps tab IDs to content ports. The content port is registered when the content script first connects. If the content script hasn't connected yet, there's no port.

The fix: when there's no content port, use `chrome.scripting.executeScript` to inject the content script into the target tab, then send the scan request. 

The content script is already loaded as a persistent content script on all pages, so it should have already connected unless the tab hasn't triggered any message yet. The real problem is that the content script only registers with the background when it first connects, but if the tab is inactive or hasn't been navigated yet, that registration never happens. I need to ensure the content script is injected into the target tab and wait for it to connect before sending the scan request.

The solution is to inject the content script programmatically using `chrome.scripting.executeScript` when the content port doesn't exist. This way the script gets injected, connects to the background, and then the background can forward the scan request to it.

The user mentioned it's an IDP-initiated login, so I need to trace through the SAML POST flow to see where it's breaking down.

Looking at the flow: the content script should capture the SAML POST form submission via the submit event listener, which sends the event to the background script. That should normalize the event and run the rules, then broadcast the updated session back to the panel. But if nothing's showing up, either the form submission isn't being caught, the message isn't reaching the background, or the normalization and rules aren't producing any findings.

For IdP-initiated flows, the real issue is likely that the SAMLResponse POST happens to a URL that isn't being captured by the normalizers. The content script should be intercepting the form submission and creating a RawCaptureEvent, but if the normalized event isn't being processed correctly or the rules aren't generating findings from it, the session won't update with any results.

Let me trace through what's actually being captured. The webRequest listeners should catch navigation requests to the IdP and SP endpoints, while the content script intercepts the form POST itself. So both should be contributing events to the session.

But I'm wondering if the real issue is that the service worker is shutting down before the login completes. The keepalive timer only pings every 25 seconds, and if the SW terminates after the user clicks Start but before the IdP-initiated login finishes, the session gets wiped from memory. Though with the persistence layer in place, it should recover the active session on restart. But maybe there's a timing issue where the events aren't being processed in the right order.

Or maybe the problem is even simpler - the user needs to click "Use current tab" first, and the service worker is still shutting down between that action and when they click Start, so the messagingTabId gets reset.

Actually, I think the real issue might be that the webRequest listeners aren't capturing the requests at all, or the events aren't being normalized properly into SAML or OIDC events, or the rules simply aren't generating findings from whatever's being captured.

The most practical fixes would be to first add a clear status message in the UI that shows when the service worker is active versus when it's shut down, and second, log what events are actually being captured so we can see if the normalizer is recognizing them. The `<>` at line 409 is a fragment wrapper for the fix tab's true branch. But the `detailTab === "fix" ?` opened at line 409 with `{detailTab === "fix" ? (`. And the `<>` is INSIDE the ternary. So the ternary's true branch IS the `<>` fragment. When the fragment closes at `</>`, the ternary's true branch also ends. But the ternary expression needs a `)` to close.

So the structure should be:
```
{detailTab === "fix" ? (
  <>
    <PreFlightChecklist />
    <div className="sections">
      {recipe.sections.map(...)}
    </div>
    <div className="section">VERIFY...</div>
  </>     ← closes the fragment (but NOT the ternary's true branch)
) : null}  ← closes the ternary
```

Wait, that doesn't work either. When you have `{condition ? ( ... ) : null}`, the `(` is part of the ternary expression syntax, not a JSX element. So:
- `{detailTab === "fix" ? ( <> content </> ) : null}` - the `(` opens the expression, and `)` closes it. The `<>` fragment is inside.

So the fix tab's structure is:
```
{detailTab === "fix" ? (
  <>
    content
  </>   ← closes fragment, NOT the ternary
) : null}   ← closes the ternary
```

So I need to trace where the closing `)` for the ternary is. The `<>` fragment is just wrapping JSX content inside the ternary expression.

--- Part 2155 ---

## Goal

Build **KZero Passwordless SSO Tracer**, a production-ready Chrome MV3 extension for KZero Passwordless MSP admins to diagnose SAML and OIDC SSO problems. The extension captures federation traffic, decodes artifacts, runs deterministic diagnostic rules, maps findings to KZero UI field labels, and provides guided fix steps.

## Instructions

- TypeScript, clean modular architecture, no heavy frameworks beyond React
- MV3: service worker, DevTools panel, Side Panel, popup window, content scripts
- KZero brand color is `#f85c3a` (orange); extension icons generated from SVG at build time via `@resvg/resvg-js`
- Redact secrets by default; local-first; no external transmission
- Deterministic rules engine first (not AI); deterministic Fix Recipes per rule ID mapped to KZero field labels
- Never mention "Keycloak" or "Multi-Pass" — always "KZero Passwordless"
- Extension must work via Side Panel ("Use current tab" targeting) and via popup window ("Pop out")
- `webRequest` capture must work globally (not just DevTools-inspected tab)

## Discoveries

- The extension UI was rebuilt from a clunky "lists + raw JSON" layout into a 3-pane diagnostics console with tabs and "Fix Steps" as the default detail view
- Deterministic **Fix Recipe** layer added — each finding generates procedural steps: KZero field labels, expected values, vendor checks, verification steps, copy helpers
- **Product UI field scanning** implemented: content script scans visible form fields on the target tab using label matching + alias expansion; panel shows Current vs Expected table; "Locate" scroll-highlights fields
- Side Panel width is a Chrome-level constraint — the panel always consumes horizontal space. Extension has narrow single-pane tab layout + "Pop out" button for separate window
- `isPopup` / `isNarrow` / `narrowTab` / `openPopup` state variables were referenced in narrow-layout JSX but their `useState` declarations were missing — caused `ReferenceError` in Side Panel — fixed
- `#f85c3a` orange branding applied to extension icon (SVG → PNG at build), primary button, LIVE badge, gradient accent line
- All "Multi-Pass" and "Keycloak" strings removed from UI copy, rule titles, README
- **Critical capture bug #1**: `webRequest.onCompleted` was missing from background — only `onErrorOccurred` was present, so successful auth requests (200s, 302s) were silently ignored
- **Critical capture bug #2**: Side Panel "Start" sent `START_CAPTURE` for `tabId = -1` while webRequest events came in with the real target tab id — sessions never matched. Fixed by routing "Start" through `effectiveTabId`
- **Critical capture bug #3**: `"fetch"` is not a valid `webRequest` type — caused service worker registration failure (status 15). Removed it from filter, kept `xmlhttprequest`
- **`toHeaderMap` crash**: headers can be `undefined` in `onCompleted` callbacks — fixed with null guard
- **Panel port routing bug**: panel registered its port under `tabId = -1` (since `getTabId("sidepanel")` always returns -1), but `sendAction` sent messages with the real tab ID. Fixed by introducing `messagingTabId` state and reordering initialization to resolve tab first, then register port
- **Service worker keepalive**: MV3 SWs shut down aggressively. Replaced `setInterval` with `chrome.alarms.create("keepalive", {periodInMinutes: 0.25})` — designed specifically for SW lifecycle management
- **Session persistence across SW restarts**: `sessions` map is in-memory and wiped on SW restart. Added `sessionCache` + top-level `chrome.storage.local.get(null)` to restore active sessions when SW wakes up
- **Bfcache disconnect errors**: Chrome moves panel page into back/forward cache when idle, closing the message port. Added `port.onDisconnect` cleanup. The warning is benign
- **Content script `form.submit()` interception**: IdP-initiated logins typically auto-submit via `HTMLFormElement.prototype.submit()` which bypasses the `submit` event listener. Added `HTMLFormElement.prototype.submit` override + early DOM scan + MutationObserver
- **Check fields fails silently**: when content script hadn't connected yet, `contentPorts` had no entry and `REQUEST_UI_SCAN` returned error. Fixed by injecting on demand via `chrome.scripting.executeScript` with `scripting` permission
- **Findings were too technical**: rule IDs shown everywhere, severity labels were "ERROR"/"INFO" (meaningless to non-technical users), detail view defaulted to "Fix Steps" which was often empty
- **"Realm" → "Tenant"**: all user-facing text and variable names updated. URL patterns like `/realms/` kept as-is since they match KZero's actual URL structure
- **JSX editing corruption**: multiple rounds of fragmented edits caused cascading structural issues (missing fragments, orphaned closing tags, corrupted PreFlightChecklist definition). The file is currently in a broken state and won't compile

## Accomplished

### Core extension
- Project scaffold: `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `scripts/build.mjs`, `scripts/clean.mjs`, `scripts/generate-icons.mjs`
- MV3 manifest, service worker, DevTools entry, content scripts, Side Panel entry, popup support
- `src/capture/sessionStore.ts` — per-tab session store with `chrome.storage.local` persistence and history
- `src/normalizers/index.ts` — raw event → NormalizedEvent (SAML/OIDC/network detection + field extraction)
- `src/parsers/saml.ts` — base64 + DEFLATE decode + XML parse with fast-xml-parser + claim extraction
- `src/parsers/jwt.ts` — JWT header/payload decode
- `src/parsers/oidc.ts` — OIDC callback/token artifact extraction
- `src/rules/samlRules.ts` — ~18 deterministic SAML checks
- `src/rules/oidcRules.ts` — ~18 deterministic OIDC checks
- `src/rules/crossRules.ts` — cross-cutting checks
- `src/mappings/fieldMappings.ts` — KZero → vendor field label mapping
- `src/mappings/uiLabelAliases.ts` — label alias expansion for UI field scanning
- `src/rules/index.ts` — findings engine with deduplication
- `src/export/sanitizedExport.ts` — sanitized JSON export
- `src/shared/redaction.ts` — secret/identifier masking
- `src/shared/ruleCatalog.ts` — per-rule documentation
- `src/recipes/types.ts`, `src/recipes/context.ts`, `src/recipes/buildRecipe.ts` — Fix Recipe system with 12+ rule implementations
- `src/panel/KZeroLogo.tsx` — embedded KZero wordmark SVG
- `src/panel/App.tsx` — React panel (3-pane + narrow single-pane tab layout, timeline, findings, detail tabs, DiffLine, Key Fields table, XPath inspector, Check fields table, rule ID filter, text search)
- `src/panel/styles.css` — dark theme with `#f85c3a` orange accents, gradient accent line
- `src/static/icon.svg` — branded extension icon
- `src/fixtures/oidc-redirect-mismatch.json`, `src/fixtures/saml-audience-mismatch.json`
- `tests/jwt.test.ts`, `tests/saml.test.ts`, `tests/rules.test.ts` — 5 tests passing
- `README.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`

### Features added across rounds
- Rule ID catalog filter + per-rule docs
- SAML XML XPath inspector with highlights
- Side Panel support with session history persistence
- Fix Recipes with KZero field expectations + verify checklists
- Product UI field scanner (content script → background → panel) with "Check fields" and "Locate"
- UI label alias support
- Side Panel "Pop out" to separate popup window via `chrome.windows.create()`
- Narrow-width single-pane tab layout for panel
- Extension icons generated from SVG at build time
- Global `webRequest.onCompleted` capture (fixed missing listener bug)
- Side Panel "Start" routing through `effectiveTabId` (fixed tab id routing bug)
- Global `onBeforeRequest` added for main_frame/sub_frame with POST body capture
- `webRequest` type filter removed from `onCompleted` (no longer filter by type)
- `chrome.alarms` keepalive for service worker persistence
- Session store restores active sessions from storage on SW startup
- Content script injects on demand for Check fields (via `chrome.scripting.executeScript`)
- `form.submit()` interception + DOM scan + MutationObserver for IdP-initiated login capture
- `toHeaderMap` handles undefined headers
- Port disconnect handler for bfcache errors
- "Realm" → "Tenant" global rename
- Severity renamed: ERROR→Problem, WARNING→Warning, INFO→Notice
- Severity legend added to findings list
- Rule IDs removed from findings list rows, detail headers, and buttons
- Default detail tab changed from "Fix Steps" to "What Happened"
- Onboarding state redesigned with 3-step guide for new users
- LIVE banner with pulsing dot added for active capture state
- "No issues found" state with friendly message
- `PreFlightChecklist` component added (shows KZero + Vendor fields to check)
- `PreFlightChecklist` added to both "Fix Steps" and "What Happened" tabs in both layouts

### Currently broken — JSX structural corruption in `src/panel/App.tsx`
The file has cascading JSX structural errors from fragmented edit attempts. Multiple closing fragments `</>`, ternary expression bodies, and divs are misaligned. The build currently fails with: `src/panel/App.tsx:249:16: ERROR: Unexpected ")"`. The `PreFlightChecklist` component was partially overwritten and had to be re-defined. The narrow layout's detail section and the wide layout's detail section both have broken nesting. A full structural review and repair of `src/panel/App.tsx` is needed.

### Not yet tested / in progress
- Zoho OIDC login flow capture via Side Panel "Use current tab" — needs real-world test
- Verify the full findings pipeline works on actual Zoho flow
- Verify IdP-initiated login capture works (via `form.submit()` + MutationObserver)
- Verify Check fields injection on demand works
- Verify session persistence across SW restarts

            var element = {

              $$typeof: REACT_ELEMENT_TYPE,
              // Built-in properties that belong on the element
              type,
              key,
              ref,

              // Record the component responsible for creating this element.

                value: source

                Object.freeze(element);
              }

          function jsxDEV(type, config, maybeKey, source, self) {
            {
              var propName;
              var props = {};
              var key = null;
              var ref = null;
              if (maybeKey !== void 0) {
                {
                  checkKeyStringCoercion(maybeKey);
                }
                key = "" + maybeKey;
              }
              if (hasValidKey(config)) {
                {
                  checkKeyStringCoercion(config.key);
                }
                key = "" + config.key;
              }
              if (hasValidRef(config)) {
                ref = config.ref;
                warnIfStringRefCannotBeAutoConverted(config, self);
              }
              for (propName in config) {
                if (hasOwnProperty.call(config, propName) && !RESERVED_PROPS.hasOwnProperty(propName)) {
                  props[propName] = config[propName];
                }
              }
              if (type && type.defaultProps) {
                var defaultProps = type.defaultProps;
                for (propName in defaultProps) {
                  if (props[propName] === void 0) {
                    props[propName] = defaultProps[propName];
                  }
                }
              }

                  defineKeyPropWarningGetter(props, displayName);
                }
                if (ref) {
                  defineRefPropWarningGetter(props, displayName);
                }
              }

            }

          var ReactCurrentOwner$1 = ReactSharedInternals.ReactCurrentOwner;
          var ReactDebugCurrentFrame$1 = ReactSharedInternals.ReactDebugCurrentFrame;
          function setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(element) {
            {
