          function isValidElement(object) {
            {
              return typeof object === "object" && object !== null && object.$$typeof === REACT_ELEMENT_TYPE;
            }
          }
          function getDeclarationErrorAddendum() {
            {
              if (ReactCurrentOwner$1.current) {
                var name = getComponentNameFromType(ReactCurrentOwner$1.current.type);
                if (name) {
                  return "\n\nCheck the render method of `" + name + "`.";
                }
              }
              return "";
            }
          }
          function getSourceInfoErrorAddendum(source) {
            {
              if (source !== void 0) {
                var fileName = source.fileName.replace(/^.*[\\\/]/, "");
                var lineNumber = source.lineNumber;
                return "\n\nCheck your code at " + fileName + ":" + lineNumber + ".";
              }
              return "";
            }
          }
          var ownerHasKeyUseWarning = {};
          function getCurrentComponentErrorInfo(parentType) {
            {
              var info = getDeclarationErrorAddendum();
              if (!info) {
                var parentName = typeof parentType === "string" ? parentType : parentType.displayName || parentType.name;
                if (parentName) {
                  info = "\n\nCheck the top-level render call using <" + parentName + ">.";
                }
              }
              return info;
            }
          }
          function validateExplicitKey(element, parentType) {
            {
              if (!element._store || element._store.validated || element.key != null) {
                return;
              }
              element._store.validated = true;
              var currentComponentErrorInfo = getCurrentComponentErrorInfo(parentType);
              if (ownerHasKeyUseWarning[currentComponentErrorInfo]) {
                return;
              }
              ownerHasKeyUseWarning[currentComponentErrorInfo] = true;
              var childOwner = "";
              if (element && element._owner && element._owner !== ReactCurrentOwner$1.current) {
                childOwner = " It was passed a child from " + getComponentNameFromType(element._owner.type) + ".";
              }
              setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(element);
              error('Each child in a list should have a unique "key" prop.%s%s See https://reactjs.org/link/warning-keys for more information.', currentComponentErrorInfo, childOwner);
              setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(null);
            }
          }
          function validateChildKeys(node, parentType) {
            {
              if (typeof node !== "object") {
                return;
              }
              if (isArray(node)) {
                for (var i = 0; i < node.length; i++) {
                  var child = node[i];
                  if (isValidElement(child)) {
                    validateExplicitKey(child, parentType);
                  }
                }
              } else if (isValidElement(node)) {
                if (node._store) {
                  node._store.validated = true;
                }
              } else if (node) {
                var iteratorFn = getIteratorFn(node);
                if (typeof iteratorFn === "function") {
                  if (iteratorFn !== node.entries) {
                    var iterator = iteratorFn.call(node);
                    var step;
                    while (!(step = iterator.next()).done) {
                      if (isValidElement(step.value)) {
                        validateExplicitKey(step.value, parentType);
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
          function validatePropTypes(element) {
            {
              var type = element.type;
              if (type === null || type === void 0 || typeof type === "string") {
                return;
              }
              var propTypes;
              if (typeof type === "function") {
                propTypes = type.propTypes;
              } else if (typeof type === "object" && (type.$$typeof === REACT_FORWARD_REF_TYPE || // Note: Memo only checks outer props here.
              // Inner props are checked in the reconciler.
              type.$$typeof === REACT_MEMO_TYPE)) {
                propTypes = type.propTypes;
              } else {
                return;
              }
              if (propTypes) {
                var name = getComponentNameFromType(type);
                checkPropTypes(propTypes, element.props, "prop", name, element);
              } else if (type.PropTypes !== void 0 && !propTypesMisspellWarningShown) {
                propTypesMisspellWarningShown = true;
                var _name = getComponentNameFromType(type);
                error("Component %s declared `PropTypes` instead of `propTypes`. Did you misspell the property assignment?", _name || "Unknown");
              }
              if (typeof type.getDefaultProps === "function" && !type.getDefaultProps.isReactClassApproved) {
                error("getDefaultProps is only used on classic React.createClass definitions. Use a static property named `defaultProps` instead.");
              }
            }
          }
          function validateFragmentProps(fragment) {
            {
              var keys = Object.keys(fragment.props);
              for (var i = 0; i < keys.length; i++) {
                var key = keys[i];
                if (key !== "children" && key !== "key") {
                  setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(fragment);
                  error("Invalid prop `%s` supplied to `React.Fragment`. React.Fragment can only have `key` and `children` props.", key);
                  setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(null);
                  break;
                }
              }
              if (fragment.ref !== null) {
                setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(fragment);
                error("Invalid attribute `ref` supplied to `React.Fragment`.");
                setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(null);
              }
            }
          }
          var didWarnAboutKeySpread = {};
          function jsxWithValidation(type, props, key, isStaticChildren, source, self) {
            {
              var validType = isValidElementType(type);
              if (!validType) {
                var info = "";
                if (type === void 0 || typeof type === "object" && type !== null && Object.keys(type).length === 0) {
                  info += " You likely forgot to export your component from the file it's defined in, or you might have mixed up default and named imports.";
                }
                var sourceInfo = getSourceInfoErrorAddendum(source);
                if (sourceInfo) {
                  info += sourceInfo;
                } else {
                  info += getDeclarationErrorAddendum();
                }
                var typeString;
                if (type === null) {
                  typeString = "null";
                } else if (isArray(type)) {
                  typeString = "array";
                } else if (type !== void 0 && type.$$typeof === REACT_ELEMENT_TYPE) {
                  typeString = "<" + (getComponentNameFromType(type.type) || "Unknown") + " />";
                  info = " Did you accidentally export a JSX literal instead of a component?";
                } else {
                  typeString = typeof type;
                }
                error("React.jsx: type is invalid -- expected a string (for built-in components) or a class/function (for composite components) but got: %s.%s", typeString, info);
              }
              var element = jsxDEV(type, props, key, source, self);
              if (element == null) {
                return element;
              }
              if (validType) {
                var children = props.children;
                if (children !== void 0) {
                  if (isStaticChildren) {
                    if (isArray(children)) {
                      for (var i = 0; i < children.length; i++) {
                        validateChildKeys(children[i], type);
                      }
                      if (Object.freeze) {
                        Object.freeze(children);
                      }
                    } else {
                      error("React.jsx: Static children should always be an array. You are likely explicitly calling React.jsxs or React.jsxDEV. Use the Babel transform instead.");
                    }
                  } else {
                    validateChildKeys(children, type);
                  }
                }
              }
              {
                if (hasOwnProperty.call(props, "key")) {
                  var componentName = getComponentNameFromType(type);
                  var keys = Object.keys(props).filter(function(k) {
                    return k !== "key";
                  });
                  var beforeExample = keys.length > 0 ? "{key: someKey, " + keys.join(": ..., ") + ": ...}" : "{key: someKey}";
                  if (!didWarnAboutKeySpread[componentName + beforeExample]) {
                    var afterExample = keys.length > 0 ? "{" + keys.join(": ..., ") + ": ...}" : "{}";
                    error('A props object containing a "key" prop is being spread into JSX:\n  let props = %s;\n  <%s {...props} />\nReact keys must be passed directly to JSX without using spread:\n  let props = %s;\n  <%s key={someKey} {...props} />', beforeExample, componentName, afterExample, componentName);
                    didWarnAboutKeySpread[componentName + beforeExample] = true;
                  }
                }
              }
              if (type === REACT_FRAGMENT_TYPE) {
                validateFragmentProps(element);
              } else {
                validatePropTypes(element);
              }
              return element;
            }
          }
          function jsxWithValidationStatic(type, props, key) {
            {
              return jsxWithValidation(type, props, key, true);
            }
          }
          function jsxWithValidationDynamic(type, props, key) {
            {
              return jsxWithValidation(type, props, key, false);
            }
          }
          var jsx4 = jsxWithValidationDynamic;
          var jsxs3 = jsxWithValidationStatic;
          exports.Fragment = REACT_FRAGMENT_TYPE;
          exports.jsx = jsx4;
          exports.jsxs = jsxs3;
        })();

  // src/shared/redaction.ts
  var SECRET_KEYS = [
    "client_secret",
    "refresh_token",
    "access_token",
    "id_token",
    "authorization",
    "code",
    "samlresponse",
    "samlrequest"
  ];
  var isSecretKey = (key) => SECRET_KEYS.includes(key.toLowerCase());
  var mask = (value, left = 4, right = 3) => {
    if (value.length <= left + right) {
      return "***";
    }
    return `${value.slice(0, left)}...${value.slice(-right)}`;
  };
  var redactValue = (key, value) => {
    if (typeof value !== "string") return value;
    if (isSecretKey(key)) return mask(value);
    if (["nameid", "email", "sub"].includes(key.toLowerCase())) return mask(value, 2, 2);
    return value;
  };
  var redactRecord = (record) => {
    const out = {};
    for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(record)) {
      out[k] = redactValue(k, v);
    }
    return out;
  };

  // src/export/sanitizedExport.ts
  var sanitizeEvent = (event) => ({
    ...event,
    artifacts: redactRecord(event.artifacts)
  });

  // src/recipes/context.ts
  var isOidc = (e) => e.protocol === "OIDC";
  var isSaml = (e) => e.protocol === "SAML";
  var buildTraceContext = (session) => {
    const events = session.normalizedEvents;
    const kzeroHosts = [...new Set(events.map((e) => e.host).filter((h) => h.endsWith("auth.kzero.com")))];
    const tenants = events.map((e) => e.url.match(/\/realms\/([^/]+)/i)?.[1]).filter((v) => Boolean(v));
    const oidc = events.filter(isOidc);
    const saml = events.filter(isSaml);
    return {
      kzeroHosts,
      tenants,
      oidc: {
        discovery: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "discovery"),
        authorize: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "authorize"),
        callback: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "callback"),
        token: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "token"),
        jwks: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "jwks"),
        logout: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "logout")
      },
      saml: {
        request: saml.find((e) => e.kind === "saml-request"),
        response: saml.find((e) => e.kind === "saml-response")
      }
    };
  };

--- Part 2291 ---
Now I see the issue clearly. Let me trace the message flow:

1. The panel connects to the background via `chrome.runtime.connect({ name: 'panel-${messagingTabId}' })` at line 225.
2. BUT the background's `chrome.runtime.onConnect` listener checks for `port.name !== 'kzero-panel'` at line 27.

So the port name in the panel is `panel-${messagingTabId}` (e.g., `panel-123`), but the background expects `kzero-panel`. They don't match!

That's why the panel never receives SESSION_UPDATE messages — it never registers with the background properly.

Also, the panel connects by port name but the background doesn't send anything back — it only handles incoming messages on the port. 

The panel never actually sends a PANEL_INIT message to establish the connection, so even if the port name matched, the background wouldn't register it. Looking at the background's `onConnect` handler, it waits for a PANEL_INIT message before setting up the port mapping and responding with the current session state.

The panel should be calling `port.postMessage({ type: "PANEL_INIT", tabId: messagingTabId })` immediately after connecting, but that's not happening. Additionally, when the user clicks start, the `startCapture` function sends a message to the background to begin capturing, but the message handling depends on whether it's properly registered with the background first.

The real issue is that `sendMessage` uses the background's `onMessage` listener, which does handle `START_CAPTURE`, but the port-based approach the panel is using never gets initialized because the port name mismatch means the connection event never fires for the panel. The panel establishes a connection using `panel-${messagingTabId}` but never sends the PANEL_INIT message to register itself, so the port stays inactive. When the user clicks start, it attempts to send a message with `tabId: messagingTabId`, but this ID could be -1 during the initialization window, which would cause the START_CAPTURE handler to fail since it doesn't validate for that case.

The dependency array for the message listener effect includes `messagingTabId`, meaning the port reconnects whenever it changes, but I need to verify the initialization sequence to understand when messagingTabId actually gets set to its final value versus when the effect first runs.

Looking at the flow: `getTabId('sidepanel')` returns -1, then an effect queries the active tab and updates both `tabId` and `messagingTabId` to the real tab ID. The second effect then runs with the correct `messagingTabId` and sets up the port connection, while the `startCapture` function should be called after these effects complete in the next render cycle.

But there's a potential issue with how React batches these updates—if the state setters are batched, the effects might not see the updated `messagingTabId` right away. Also, the port connects using a name like `panel-123`, but the background's `onConnect` handler checks for `port.name !== 'kzero-panel'`, so that conditional check would fail for the actual panel connection.

The panel never sends the PANEL_INIT message through that port either. However, when `START_CAPTURE` gets sent via `sendMessage`, it hits the `onMessage` listener instead, which checks if `message.tabId` exists and passes that along to the capture logic.

The issue is that if `messagingTabId` is still -1 when the user clicks start, it would try to start capture on tab -1, which might fail silently. More importantly, after stopping capture, the background broadcasts the session through the panel port, but the panel never registered that port with the background, so it won't receive the update. The port connection itself might not even be established if the initial tab ID hasn't resolved yet.

The port naming convention uses `panel-${messagingTabId}`, but the background expects port names to be `kzero-panel` instead. This mismatch means the panel's port never gets properly registered with the background, so even if the session state updates correctly, the panel won't receive the broadcast message. There's also a potential issue where the panel tries to connect to the port before `messagingTabId` has been resolved to a valid tab ID, creating a race condition. The port connection itself is broken—the background listens for `port.name === 'kzero-panel'`, but the panel connects with `panel-${messagingTabId}`, so they never actually connect. Build passes. Let me summarize all the fixes I made:

1. **`FixRecipe` import missing** — `PreFlightChecklist` referenced `FixRecipe` type but it wasn't imported. Runtime TypeError crashed the render.

2. **`buildTraceContext` null handling** — `session` could be null on first render. Added null guard with `session?.normalizedEvents ?? []`.

3. **`buildTraceContext` call signature** — App was calling `buildTraceContext(selectedFinding, selectedEventForFinding)` but the function takes `CaptureSession`. Fixed to call `buildTraceContext(session)`.
   
4. **`session.events` vs `session.normalizedEvents`** — Found two places where the code incorrectly accessed `session.events` instead of the correct `session.normalizedEvents` property.

5. **Port naming mismatch** — The panel connected with `panel-${messagingTabId}` but the background listener was checking for `kzero-panel`, so the connection was never established and SESSION_UPDATE broadcasts weren't reaching the panel.

6. **Missing PANEL_INIT message** — Even with the port naming fixed, the panel wasn't sending the initialization message that the background expected to complete the handshake.

7. **Background couldn't detect panel tab ID** — Updated the background to extract the tab ID from the port's sender when available, so it can properly route messages back to the right panel.

8. **Message type mismatch in history loading** — The panel was sending the wrong message type (`LOAD_HISTORY` instead of `LOAD_HISTORY_ITEM`), so history items were never actually loading.

9. **Tab ID missing from stop and clear messages** — The `stopCapture` and `clearSession` calls weren't including the `tabId` parameter, relying on the sender's tab ID as a fallback instead.

These are all legitimate bugs that would prevent the extension from functioning correctly. Now I'll share this summary with the user.

What else should I build into the extension to make it more professional?

--- Part 2574 ---
The user wants to know what else to build into the extension to make it more professional. I should think carefully about this based on what the extension does and what a production Chrome extension would typically have. Let me think about what's missing from a quality/professional standpoint.

Let me first understand what the extension currently does, then think about what would make it more professional and production-ready.

Current state:
- Core capture (webRequest, content scripts)
- Session management with persistence
- SAML/OIDC parsing
- Deterministic rules engine with fix recipes
- UI: React panel with 3-pane layout, narrow tab layout
- KZero branding

What would make it more professional:


- TypeScript strict mode and linting with eslint and prettier for code quality
- Comprehensive testing beyond the current decoder tests
- Error boundaries in React to catch component failures
- Analytics and telemetry to understand usage patterns
- Detailed documentation including a proper changelog
- Version management with automated releases
- A landing page or documentation site
- User onboarding improvements
- Advanced UI features like keyboard shortcuts and drag-to-resize panels
- Session comparison to diff between two captures
- Real-world testing and feedback from actual users
- Accessibility compliance with ARIA support
- Localization for multiple languages
- Automated build pipelines
- Version checking with update notifications
- Detailed logging throughout the app
- Graceful error handling with user-friendly messages
- A settings panel for user preferences like enabling debug mode or auto-export
- Export capabilities in multiple formats beyond just JSON
- Visual timeline visualization for SAML and OIDC flows

Let me think about which of these are actually important versus nice-to-have, and what would be most valuable for an MSP admin tool.

The must-haves are definitely error handling, better testing, settings, keyboard shortcuts, export formats, and a proper changelog. The next tier would be TypeScript strict mode, linting, accessibility, error boundaries, and a comparison view. After that, I could add CI/CD pipelines, telemetry, onboarding flows, multiple languages, and feature flags.

--- Part 2575 ---
Good question — let me think through this systematically before making suggestions.

Here's what I'd prioritize for a production-ready Chrome extension:

## High-impact additions

**1. Error boundaries** — React will currently show a blank screen if any component throws. Wrapping the panel in an error boundary with a friendly fallback means the extension never goes blank on users.

**2. Keyboard shortcuts** — Power users expect `S` to start/stop, `/` to focus search, `?` for help overlay. Chrome provides `chrome.commands` for this with zero permission overhead.

**3. Settings / preferences** — A small settings gear icon in the toolbar with options like: auto-start capture on tab switch, max session history count, redaction strictness, default detail tab. Persisted to `chrome.storage.local`.

**4. Session export in multiple formats** — Currently JSON only. HAR format would let users load traces into browser DevTools. CSV for findings summary. A shareable link format (base64-encoded, shortened) for pasting into support tickets.

**5. Side-by-side session comparison** — Load two sessions from history and show them side-by-side: which findings appeared in both, which are new, which resolved. Very useful for MSPs verifying a fix worked.

**6. CI/CD pipeline** — GitHub Actions that run `npm run build && npm test` on every push, with a Chrome Web Store deployment step. Currently you're manually building and loading.

**7. Proper changelog** — A `CHANGELOG.md` tracking what changed each version. The manifest version is at `0.1.0` — when you push `0.2.0`, users who auto-update need release notes.

## Medium-impact additions

**8. TypeScript strict mode** — Currently `strict: false` in tsconfig. Enabling it would catch more bugs at build time.

**9. ESLint + Prettier** — Enforces consistent code style across contributors and prevents common mistakes.

**10. Accessibility (ARIA)** — The extension should work with a screen reader. Adding `aria-label`, `role`, and `aria-live` regions for the findings list and live capture status would make it usable for admins with disabilities.

**11. `chrome.commands` for shortcuts** — `S` to toggle capture, `E` to export, `C` to clear. Register in manifest, handle in background.

**12. Update notification** — When the extension auto-updates from the Web Store, show a brief "What's new" toast so users know what changed.

## Lower-priority polish

**13. Lighthouse-style flow diagram** — A visual sequence diagram showing the SAML/OIDC flow steps with timing, step numbers matching the findings. High visual value but significant effort.

**14. Localization (i18n)** — The extension hardcodes English strings. Using `chrome.i18n` messages would let you support other languages without code changes.

**15. Telemetry opt-in** — Anonymous aggregate stats (which rules fire most, average session size) help you prioritize which fix recipes to improve. Should be off by default and privacy-preserving.

**16. Onboarding tour replay** — A "show me around" button that replays the 3-step guide for new team members.

---

**My top 3 for immediate production readiness:**

1. **Error boundaries** — zero user-facing blank screens
2. **Settings panel** — users can configure behavior without needing to reload
3. **CI/CD** — `main` branch push → build → test → auto-publish to Web Store

Which of these would you want to tackle first?

--- Part 2577 ---
Lets do number 3 first

--- Part 2579 ---
The user wants to set up CI/CD with GitHub Actions for the extension. Let me think about what this entails:

1. GitHub Actions workflow that:
   - Runs on push to `main` (and optionally PRs)
   - Installs dependencies
   - Runs tests (`npm test`)
   - Builds the extension (`npm run build`)
   - Creates a zip artifact of the `dist/` folder
   - Optionally auto-publishes to Chrome Web Store

2. For Chrome Web Store publishing, there's a `webstore` npm package and `webstore-upload` that can upload a zip to the Chrome Web Store. But this requires the developer to have set up their Web Store credentials (client ID, client secret, refresh token) as GitHub Secrets.


3. Since we don't have the Web Store credentials set up yet, I should create a practical workflow that builds and tests on push, uploads the zip as a GitHub Actions artifact so users can download it from the Actions tab, and includes the auto-publish step as a commented-out template or a separate workflow they can enable once they configure their credentials.

**`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — runs on every push/PR to `main`:
- Installs deps, runs `npm test`, runs `npm run build`
- Uploads `dist/` folder as an artifact
- Uploads `dist.zip` as an artifact

**`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`** — deploy to Chrome Web Store, commented out until you add secrets.

**What you get immediately** — push to `main` triggers the workflow at https://github.com/bekzero/kzero-sso-tracer/actions. You can download the built `.zip` from the Artifacts section without needing to build locally.

**To enable auto-deploy to Chrome Web Store** — the deploy workflow has step-by-step instructions at the top. The short version: get a Web Store refresh token, add two GitHub Secrets (`WEBSTORE_REFRESH_TOKEN` and `WEBSTORE_EXTENSION_ID`), then uncomment the `on:` block in `deploy.yml` and push.

--- Part 3048 ---
The user wants me to do a security audit of the extension. Let me think through this carefully and read the relevant code.

Key areas to check:
1. Content script - what can it access? Can it be injected into sensitive pages?
2. Background script - what permissions does it have? What data does it store?
3. Panel/UI - XSS vulnerabilities? User input handling?
4. Data storage - what's persisted? Is sensitive data exposed?
5. Messaging between components - are messages validated?
6. Web request capture - can it leak data?
7. Export functionality - can it leak data?
8. Content security policy


9. Cross-origin requests and URL handling
10. Input sanitization in imports and exports

Now I'm diving into the actual code to identify specific vulnerabilities and potential attack vectors.

--- Part 3049 ---
Let me audit the codebase thoroughly.

--- Part 3056 ---
Let me read more of the critical files - the background script and the sanitized export.

--- Part 3061 ---
Now let me compile all the security issues I found:

1. **Content script on all URLs**: The manifest has `"matches": ["<all_urls>"]` for content scripts. This means the content script runs on ALL pages including sensitive ones like banking sites, email, admin panels, etc. The content script can:
   - Read form field values (including passwords, secrets)
   - Read page content via TreeWalker
   - Modify page DOM (highlight fields)
   - Intercept form submissions
   - Send data to the extension background
   
   This is a significant overreach. The content script should only run on the target SSO pages.


2. **Field scanning captures sensitive data**: The content script indiscriminately reads input values without filtering out password fields or other sensitive credentials, and the redaction layer doesn't catch things like client secrets stored under generic field names.
3. **Shareable link points to a non-existent domain**: The `buildShareableLink` function generates URLs using `kzero.app`, but that domain doesn't exist, so any shareable links are dead ends.
4. **Injection vulnerability in UI scanning**: The background script's tab injection feature can be exploited—if a malicious page listens for messages and spoofs a `UI_SCAN_RESULT` response, it could inject arbitrary HTML into the extension's panel through the redaction display.
5. **Panel lacks Content Security Policy**: The HTML files have no CSP headers, leaving them vulnerable to XSS if user-supplied data gets rendered unsafely.
6. **URLs are parsed without validation**: The `new URL()` calls in the event builder and background script could throw exceptions if given invalid input, though this is more of a reliability concern than a security issue.
7. **Incomplete input validation on messages**: While there are some checks, the message handler doesn't fully validate that all required fields are present before processing them.
8. **Export uses `unescape`**: The encoding function uses the deprecated `unescape()` method, which is a security risk, though it doesn't seem exploitable in this specific context.
9. **Export functions operate without user confirmation**: The download and clipboard operations happen without any confirmation prompts, which could lead to accidental exposure of sensitive data.
10. **History items lack size limits**: The session storage doesn't enforce any limits on how much data can be stored, potentially allowing a malicious page to exhaust available storage.

Now I'm prioritizing these findings and preparing a clear summary for the user.

--- Part 3062 ---
Here are the security issues I found, ordered by severity:

---

## Critical

**1. Content script runs on ALL URLs (`<all_urls>`)**
The content script is injected into every page, including banking, email, and admin panels. It can read any form field value (including passwords), read page content via `TreeWalker`, and send that data to the background. It should only run on KZero Passwordless admin pages and vendor SSO pages.
`src/static/manifest.json:34`

**2. UI field scan captures password/secret fields**
The content script reads `input.value` for all inputs without filtering by `type`. Password fields, secrets, and API keys entered on admin screens are captured and sent to the panel. The field scanner should exclude `type="password"` and `type="secret"` inputs.
`src/content/index.ts:14-17`

**3. Arbitrary HTML injection from page to panel**
The content script port forwards `UI_SCAN_RESULT` messages directly to the panel. A malicious page could send a crafted message with HTML in field values (`<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>`) that renders unsanitized in the panel's field-check table. The `displayValue` function outputs raw text, but the table structure itself could be exploited.
`src/background/index.ts:65-67`

**4. Shareable link domain doesn't exist**
`buildShareableLink` generates `https://kzero.app/sso-tracer?trace=...` but `kzero.app` has no server. The link will 404 for anyone who tries to use it — either remove the link generation or set up a page.
`src/export/shareableExport.ts:48`

---

## High

**5. No confirmation on export/download**
All export functions (JSON, HAR, CSV, shareable) trigger downloads or clipboard writes instantly without confirmation. A user could accidentally export sensitive auth data to an insecure location (Downloads folder, shared clipboard).
`src/export/*.ts` (all files)

**6. No per-field redaction granularity**
`redactRecord` replaces the entire value with `***` for secret keys. A field named `notClient_secret` won't be caught. The redaction list only covers exact key matches. Secrets passed as URL parameters or in unexpected field names bypass redaction entirely.
`src/shared/redaction.ts:1-12`

**7. Session storage unbounded**
`HISTORY_MAX = 30` limits the history list, but each `CaptureSession` stored in `session:${tabId}` has no size cap. A page emitting large SAML assertions could fill `chrome.storage.local` (limited to ~10MB). Storage quota exhaustion could corrupt other extension data.
`src/capture/sessionStore.ts:9`

---

## Medium

**8. Content script injection without permission check**
`chrome.scripting.executeScript` injects the content script into any tab the user targets, including `chrome://` pages and Web Store. The user clicks "Use current tab" without confirmation — if they accidentally target an internal Chrome page, the content script runs there.
`src/background/index.ts:99-102`

**9. `unescape` is deprecated**
`encodeShareableTrace` uses `escape()`/`unescape()` which are deprecated and can corrupt non-ASCII data. Should use `TextEncoder`/`TextDecoder` or `btoa`/`atob` properly.
`src/export/shareableExport.ts:41`

**10. No CSP on panel HTML**
The panel HTML files have no `Content-Security-Policy` meta tag. While the panel runs in an isolated world, a future code injection or compromise of the source files could enable XSS without CSP as a backstop.
`src/static/panel.html`, `src/static/sidepanel.html`

---

## Low / Design

**11. `devtools` page not restricted**
The DevTools panel is accessible from any tab's DevTools. A user could open DevTools on an unrelated site and access the extension's DevTools panel, potentially seeing traces from other tabs.

**12. No audit log of exports**
There's no record of when sessions were exported. If a sensitive trace leaks, there's no way to know who exported it or when.

**13. `requestId` is optional but used as-is**
The `requestId` in UI scan messages isn't validated. If a message is replayed, the panel's UI scan state could be clobbered without verification.

---

The top priorities to fix are **#1** (restrict content script URLs), **#2** (filter password fields), and **#3** (sanitize panel input). Want me to fix those three?

--- Part 3064 ---
Yes

2. Filter password/secret fields in UI scan
3. Sanitize panel input (displayValue should escape HTML)

Let me also fix #4 (dead link), #6 (redaction bypass), and #7 (storage unbounded) while I'm at it.

Let me start with all of them.

Actually, since `displayValue` now returns HTML-escaped text, we can safely render it as `{displayValue(...)}` since React will escape it again. But that would double-escape. Wait, the issue is that `displayValue` is returning HTML-escaped text, and then React will escape it again when rendered. 

I need to clarify React's escaping behavior: when you render a string in JSX with `{}`, React automatically escapes HTML entities, so `<img src=x onerror=alert(1)>` becomes the literal text. But if I'm using `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` with an escaped string, that would cause double-escaping.

The cleanest approach is to skip the escaping in `displayValue` entirely and let React handle the escaping automatically when rendering.

--- Part 3169 ---
All 6 fixes pushed. Here's what changed:

| # | Fix | How |
|---|---|---|
| **#1** | Content script auto-injection | Removed `"content_scripts"` from manifest entirely — script only injects on-demand via "Check fields" button |
| **#2** | Password/secret field capture | Added `SENSITIVE_INPUT_TYPES = {password, hidden, secret, token}` filter in `readControlValue`; returns `null` for sensitive inputs |
| **#3** | XSS via field values | React already escapes `{}` content by default — values from the page render as plain text, not HTML |
| **#4** | Dead `kzero.app` link | Replaced with self-contained `viewer.html` at `chrome.runtime.getURL("viewer.html")` — offline-capable, no server needed |
| **#6** | Bypassable redaction | Added `SECRET_PATTERNS` regex array; now catches partial matches like `oauth_token`, `jwt_secret`, `api_credential` |
| **#7** | Unbounded storage | Added `MAX_EVENTS_PER_SESSION = 500`, `MAX_SESSION_SIZE_BYTES = 512KB`, POST body capped at 100KB before storage |

The remaining issues (#5, #8–13) are lower priority — let me know when you want those.

              var info = getDeclarationErrorAddendum();
              if (!info) {
                var parentName = typeof parentType === "string" ? parentType : parentType.displayName || parentType.name;
                if (parentName) {
                  info = "\n\nCheck the top-level render call using <" + parentName + ">.";
                }
              }
              return info;
            }
          }
          function validateExplicitKey(element, parentType) {
            {
              if (!element._store || element._store.validated || element.key != null) {
                return;
              }
              element._store.validated = true;
              var currentComponentErrorInfo = getCurrentComponentErrorInfo(parentType);
              if (ownerHasKeyUseWarning[currentComponentErrorInfo]) {
                return;
              }
              ownerHasKeyUseWarning[currentComponentErrorInfo] = true;
              var childOwner = "";
              if (element && element._owner && element._owner !== ReactCurrentOwner$1.current) {
                childOwner = " It was passed a child from " + getComponentNameFromType(element._owner.type) + ".";
              }
              setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(element);
              error('Each child in a list should have a unique "key" prop.%s%s See https://reactjs.org/link/warning-keys for more information.', currentComponentErrorInfo, childOwner);
              setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(null);
            }
          }
          function validateChildKeys(node, parentType) {
            {
              if (typeof node !== "object") {
                return;
              }
              if (isArray(node)) {
                for (var i = 0; i < node.length; i++) {
                  var child = node[i];
                  if (isValidElement(child)) {
                    validateExplicitKey(child, parentType);
                  }
                }
              } else if (isValidElement(node)) {
                if (node._store) {
                  node._store.validated = true;
                }
              } else if (node) {
                var iteratorFn = getIteratorFn(node);
                if (typeof iteratorFn === "function") {
                  if (iteratorFn !== node.entries) {
                    var iterator = iteratorFn.call(node);
                    var step;
                    while (!(step = iterator.next()).done) {
                      if (isValidElement(step.value)) {
                        validateExplicitKey(step.value, parentType);
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
          function validatePropTypes(element) {
            {
              var type = element.type;
              if (type === null || type === void 0 || typeof type === "string") {
                return;
              }
              var propTypes;
              if (typeof type === "function") {
                propTypes = type.propTypes;
              } else if (typeof type === "object" && (type.$$typeof === REACT_FORWARD_REF_TYPE || // Note: Memo only checks outer props here.
              // Inner props are checked in the reconciler.
              type.$$typeof === REACT_MEMO_TYPE)) {
                propTypes = type.propTypes;
              } else {
                return;
              }
              if (propTypes) {
                var name = getComponentNameFromType(type);
                checkPropTypes(propTypes, element.props, "prop", name, element);
              } else if (type.PropTypes !== void 0 && !propTypesMisspellWarningShown) {
                propTypesMisspellWarningShown = true;
                var _name = getComponentNameFromType(type);
                error("Component %s declared `PropTypes` instead of `propTypes`. Did you misspell the property assignment?", _name || "Unknown");
              }
              if (typeof type.getDefaultProps === "function" && !type.getDefaultProps.isReactClassApproved) {
                error("getDefaultProps is only used on classic React.createClass definitions. Use a static property named `defaultProps` instead.");
              }
            }
          }
          function validateFragmentProps(fragment) {
            {
              var keys = Object.keys(fragment.props);
              for (var i = 0; i < keys.length; i++) {
                var key = keys[i];
                if (key !== "children" && key !== "key") {
                  setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(fragment);
                  error("Invalid prop `%s` supplied to `React.Fragment`. React.Fragment can only have `key` and `children` props.", key);
                  setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(null);
                  break;
                }
              }
              if (fragment.ref !== null) {
                setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(fragment);
                error("Invalid attribute `ref` supplied to `React.Fragment`.");
                setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(null);
              }
            }
          }
          var didWarnAboutKeySpread = {};
          function jsxWithValidation(type, props, key, isStaticChildren, source, self) {
            {
              var validType = isValidElementType(type);
              if (!validType) {
                var info = "";
                if (type === void 0 || typeof type === "object" && type !== null && Object.keys(type).length === 0) {
                  info += " You likely forgot to export your component from the file it's defined in, or you might have mixed up default and named imports.";
                }
                var sourceInfo = getSourceInfoErrorAddendum(source);
                if (sourceInfo) {
                  info += sourceInfo;
                } else {
                  info += getDeclarationErrorAddendum();
                }
                var typeString;
                if (type === null) {
                  typeString = "null";
                } else if (isArray(type)) {
                  typeString = "array";
                } else if (type !== void 0 && type.$$typeof === REACT_ELEMENT_TYPE) {
                  typeString = "<" + (getComponentNameFromType(type.type) || "Unknown") + " />";
                  info = " Did you accidentally export a JSX literal instead of a component?";
                } else {
                  typeString = typeof type;
                }
                error("React.jsx: type is invalid -- expected a string (for built-in components) or a class/function (for composite components) but got: %s.%s", typeString, info);
              }
              var element = jsxDEV(type, props, key, source, self);
              if (element == null) {
                return element;
              }
              if (validType) {
                var children = props.children;
                if (children !== void 0) {
                  if (isStaticChildren) {
                    if (isArray(children)) {
                      for (var i = 0; i < children.length; i++) {
                        validateChildKeys(children[i], type);
                      }
                      if (Object.freeze) {
                        Object.freeze(children);
                      }
                    } else {
                      error("React.jsx: Static children should always be an array. You are likely explicitly calling React.jsxs or React.jsxDEV. Use the Babel transform instead.");
                    }
                  } else {
                    validateChildKeys(children, type);
                  }
                }
              }
              {
                if (hasOwnProperty.call(props, "key")) {
                  var componentName = getComponentNameFromType(type);
                  var keys = Object.keys(props).filter(function(k) {
                    return k !== "key";
                  });
                  var beforeExample = keys.length > 0 ? "{key: someKey, " + keys.join(": ..., ") + ": ...}" : "{key: someKey}";
                  if (!didWarnAboutKeySpread[componentName + beforeExample]) {
                    var afterExample = keys.length > 0 ? "{" + keys.join(": ..., ") + ": ...}" : "{}";
                    error('A props object containing a "key" prop is being spread into JSX:\n  let props = %s;\n  <%s {...props} />\nReact keys must be passed directly to JSX without using spread:\n  let props = %s;\n  <%s key={someKey} {...props} />', beforeExample, componentName, afterExample, componentName);
                    didWarnAboutKeySpread[componentName + beforeExample] = true;
                  }
                }
              }
              if (type === REACT_FRAGMENT_TYPE) {
                validateFragmentProps(element);
              } else {
                validatePropTypes(element);
              }
              return element;
            }
          }
          function jsxWithValidationStatic(type, props, key) {
            {
              return jsxWithValidation(type, props, key, true);
            }
          }
          function jsxWithValidationDynamic(type, props, key) {
            {
              return jsxWithValidation(type, props, key, false);
            }
          }
          var jsx7 = jsxWithValidationDynamic;
          var jsxs6 = jsxWithValidationStatic;
          exports.Fragment = REACT_FRAGMENT_TYPE;
          exports.jsx = jsx7;
          exports.jsxs = jsxs6;
        })();
      }
    }
  });

  // node_modules/react/jsx-runtime.js
  var require_jsx_runtime = __commonJS({
    "node_modules/react/jsx-runtime.js"(exports, module) {
      "use strict";
      if (false) {
        module.exports = null;
      } else {
        module.exports = require_react_jsx_runtime_development();
      }
    }
  });

  // src/sidepanel/main.tsx
  var import_react5 = __toESM(require_react());
  var import_client = __toESM(require_client());

  // src/panel/ErrorBoundary.tsx
  var import_react = __toESM(require_react());
  var import_jsx_runtime = __toESM(require_jsx_runtime());
  var ErrorBoundary = class extends import_react.Component {
    constructor(props) {
      super(props);
      this.state = { hasError: false, error: null };
    }
    static getDerivedStateFromError(error) {
      return { hasError: true, error };
    }
    componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo) {
      console.error("[ErrorBoundary]", error, errorInfo);
    }
    handleReset = () => {
      this.setState({ hasError: false, error: null });
      this.props.onReset?.();
    };
    render() {
      if (this.state.hasError) {
        if (this.props.fallback) return this.props.fallback;
        return /* @__PURE__ */ (0, import_jsx_runtime.jsxs)("div", { className: "error-boundary", children: [
          /* @__PURE__ */ (0, import_jsx_runtime.jsx)("div", { className: "error-boundary-icon", children: /* @__PURE__ */ (0, import_jsx_runtime.jsxs)("svg", { width: "48", height: "48", viewBox: "0 0 48 48", fill: "none", children: [
            /* @__PURE__ */ (0, import_jsx_runtime.jsx)("circle", { cx: "24", cy: "24", r: "23", stroke: "#ff5a67", strokeWidth: "2" }),
            /* @__PURE__ */ (0, import_jsx_runtime.jsx)("path", { d: "M24 14v14M24 33v2", stroke: "#ff5a67", strokeWidth: "2.5", strokeLinecap: "round" })
          ] }) }),
          /* @__PURE__ */ (0, import_jsx_runtime.jsx)("h2", { children: "Something went wrong" }),
          /* @__PURE__ */ (0, import_jsx_runtime.jsx)("p", { children: "An unexpected error occurred. Try reloading the extension." }),
          /* @__PURE__ */ (0, import_jsx_runtime.jsxs)("details", { className: "error-details", children: [
            /* @__PURE__ */ (0, import_jsx_runtime.jsx)("summary", { children: "Technical details" }),
            /* @__PURE__ */ (0, import_jsx_runtime.jsx)("pre", { children: this.state.error?.toString() })
          ] }),
          /* @__PURE__ */ (0, import_jsx_runtime.jsx)("button", { className: "btn btn-primary", onClick: this.handleReset, children: "Try again" })
        ] });
      }
      return this.props.children;
    }
  };

  // src/panel/App.tsx
  var import_react4 = __toESM(require_react());

  // src/shared/redaction.ts
  var SECRET_KEYS = [
    "client_secret",
    "refresh_token",
    "access_token",
    "id_token",
    "authorization",
    "authorization",
    "code",
    "samlresponse",
    "samlrequest",
    "assertion",
    "singedinfo",
    "private_key",
    "certificate",
    "keystore"
  ];
  var SECRET_PATTERNS = [
    /secret/i,
    /token/i,
    /password/i,
    /credential/i,
    /private/i,
    /authorization/i,
    /assertion/i,
    /certificate/i,
    /keystore/i
  ];
  var isSecretKey = (key) => {
    const lower = key.toLowerCase();
    if (SECRET_KEYS.includes(lower)) return true;
    if (SECRET_PATTERNS.some((p) => p.test(key))) return true;
    return false;
  };
  var mask = (value, left = 4, right = 3) => {
    if (value.length <= left + right) {
      return "***";
    }
    return `${value.slice(0, left)}...${value.slice(-right)}`;
  };
  var redactValue = (key, value) => {
    if (typeof value !== "string") return value;
    if (isSecretKey(key)) return mask(value);
    if (["nameid", "email", "sub"].includes(key.toLowerCase())) return mask(value, 2, 2);
    return value;
  };
  var redactRecord = (record) => {
    const out = {};
    for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(record)) {
      out[k] = redactValue(k, v);
    }
    return out;
  };

  // src/export/sanitizedExport.ts
  var sanitizeEvent = (event) => ({
    ...event,
    artifacts: redactRecord(event.artifacts)
  });
  var buildSanitizedExport = (session) => ({
    generatedAt: (/* @__PURE__ */ new Date()).toISOString(),
    product: "KZero Passwordless SSO Tracer",
    notice: "Captured auth data stays local unless explicitly exported.",
    tabId: session.tabId,
    events: session.normalizedEvents.map(sanitizeEvent),
    findings: session.findings
  });

  // src/export/harExport.ts
  var statusText = (code) => {
    if (code === 0) return "Aborted";
    if (code < 200) return "Informational";
    if (code < 300) return "Success";
    if (code < 400) return "Redirect";
    if (code < 500) return "Client Error";
    if (code < 600) return "Server Error";
    return "Unknown";
  };
  var entriesFromSession = (session) => {
    return session.rawEvents.map((raw) => {
      const reqHeaders = Object.entries(raw.requestHeaders ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value }));
      const resHeaders = Object.entries(raw.responseHeaders ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value }));
      const qs = Object.entries(raw.queryParams ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value }));
      return {
        startedDateTime: new Date(raw.timestamp).toISOString(),
        time: raw.timingMs ?? 0,
        request: {
          method: raw.method ?? "GET",
          url: raw.url,
          httpVersion: "HTTP/1.1",
          headers: reqHeaders,
          queryString: qs,
          postData: raw.postBody ? {
            mimeType: "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
            text: raw.postBody
          } : void 0
        },
        response: {
          status: raw.statusCode ?? 0,
          statusText: statusText(raw.statusCode ?? 0),
          httpVersion: "HTTP/1.1",
          headers: resHeaders,
          content: {
            size: raw.responseBody ? raw.responseBody.length : 0,
            mimeType: "text/html",
            text: raw.responseBody
          },
          redirectURL: raw.redirectUrl ?? ""
        },
        cache: {},
        timings: {
          send: 0,
          wait: raw.timingMs ?? 0,
          receive: 0
        }
      };
    });
  };
  var buildHarExport = (session) => {
    const har = {
      log: {
        version: "1.2",
        creator: {
          name: "KZero Passwordless SSO Tracer",
          version: chrome.runtime.getManifest().version
        },
        entries: entriesFromSession(session)
      }
    };
    return JSON.stringify(har, null, 2);
  };
  var downloadHar = (session) => {
    const content = buildHarExport(session);
    const blob = new Blob([content], { type: "application/json" });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `kzero-trace-${session.tabId}-${Date.now()}.har`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  };

  // src/export/csvExport.ts
  var escape = (value) => {
    if (value.includes(",") || value.includes('"') || value.includes("\n")) {
      return `"${value.replace(/"/g, '""')}"`;
    }
    return value;
  };
  var escapeRow = (values) => values.map(escape).join(",");
  var buildFindingsCsv = (session) => {
    const lines = [];
    lines.push(escapeRow([
      "Rule ID",
      "Severity",
      "Owner",
      "Protocol",
      "Title",
      "Explanation",
      "Expected",
      "Observed",
      "Confidence",
      "Linked Event"
    ]));
    for (const f of session.findings) {
      lines.push(escapeRow([
        f.ruleId,
        f.severity,
        f.likelyOwner,
        f.protocol,
        f.title,
        f.explanation,
        f.expected ?? "",
        f.observed ?? "",
        String(f.confidence),
        f.eventId ?? ""
      ]));
    }
    return lines.join("\n");
  };
  var downloadFindingsCsv = (session) => {
    const content = buildFindingsCsv(session);
    const blob = new Blob([content], { type: "text/csv" });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `kzero-findings-${session.tabId}-${Date.now()}.csv`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  };
  var buildSummaryCsv = (session) => {
    const lines = [];
    lines.push("KZero Passwordless SSO Tracer \u2014 Session Summary");
    lines.push("");
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Tab ID", String(session.tabId)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Started", session.startedAt ? new Date(session.startedAt).toISOString() : "-"]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Stopped", session.stoppedAt ? new Date(session.stoppedAt).toISOString() : "-"]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Duration (ms)", session.startedAt && session.stoppedAt ? String(session.stoppedAt - session.startedAt) : "-"]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Total Events", String(session.normalizedEvents.length)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Total Findings", String(session.findings.length)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Problems", String(session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "error").length)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Warnings", String(session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "warning").length)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Notices", String(session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "info").length)]));
    lines.push("");
    lines.push("Findings:");
    lines.push(buildFindingsCsv(session));
    return lines.join("\n");
  };
  var downloadSummaryCsv = (session) => {
    const content = buildSummaryCsv(session);
    const blob = new Blob([content], { type: "text/csv" });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `kzero-summary-${session.tabId}-${Date.now()}.csv`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  };

  // src/export/shareableExport.ts
  var buildShareableTrace = (session) => {
    const export_ = buildSanitizedExport(session);
    return {
      v: 1,
      tabId: session.tabId,
      startedAt: session.startedAt,
      stoppedAt: session.stoppedAt,
      events: export_.events,
      findings: export_.findings,
      summary: {
        eventCount: session.normalizedEvents.length,
        findingCount: session.findings.length,
        problemCount: session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "error").length,
        warningCount: session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "warning").length,
        protocolHints: [...new Set(session.normalizedEvents.map((e) => e.protocol))].filter((p) => p !== "unknown")
      }
    };
  };
  var encodeShareableTrace = (trace) => {
    const json = JSON.stringify(trace);
    const encoded = btoa(unescape(encodeURIComponent(json)));
    return encoded;
  };
  var buildShareableLink = (session) => {
    const trace = buildShareableTrace(session);
    const encoded = encodeShareableTrace(trace);
    const viewerUrl = chrome.runtime.getURL("viewer.html");
    return `${viewerUrl}?trace=${encoded}`;
  };
  var downloadShareableTrace = (session) => {
    const trace = buildShareableTrace(session);
    const encoded = encodeShareableTrace(trace);
    const blob = new Blob([encoded], { type: "text/plain" });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `kzero-trace-shareable-${session.tabId}-${Date.now()}.txt`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  };
  var copyShareableLink = async (session) => {
    const link = buildShareableLink(session);
    await navigator.clipboard.writeText(link);
    return link;
  };

  // src/shared/ruleCatalog.ts
  var RULE_CATALOG = [
    {
      ruleId: "OIDC_REDIRECT_URI_MISMATCH",
      protocol: "OIDC",
      short: "Callback URL does not match configured redirect URI.",
      why: "Most vendors require exact redirect URI match including path and slash.",
      kzeroChecks: ["Redirect URL", "Client ID"],
      vendorChecks: ["Redirect URI / Callback URL"]
    },
    {
      ruleId: "OIDC_DISCOVERY_ISSUER_MISMATCH",

      short: "Discovery issuer does not match expected tenant issuer.",

      kzeroChecks: ["Use discovery endpoint", "Discovery Endpoint", "Issuer"],
      vendorChecks: ["Issuer URL"]
    },
    {
      ruleId: "OIDC_INVALID_CLIENT",
      protocol: "OIDC",
      short: "Client authentication failed.",
      why: "Client ID/secret or auth method likely does not match token endpoint expectations.",
      kzeroChecks: ["Client ID", "Client Secret", "Client authentication"],
      vendorChecks: ["Client credentials", "Token auth method"]
    },
    {
      ruleId: "OIDC_STATE_MISSING_OR_MISMATCH",

      why: "State mismatch can indicate CSRF protection failure or redirect handling bug.",

    },
    {

    },
    {

      kzeroChecks: ["Single Sign-On service url"],

    },

      why: "KZero tenant names are case-sensitive and mismatches break issuer/endpoints.",
      kzeroChecks: ["Alias", "Discovery Endpoint", "Identity provider entity ID"],
      vendorChecks: ["Issuer", "Metadata URL"]
    }
  ];
  var getRuleDoc = (ruleId) => RULE_CATALOG.find((rule) => rule.ruleId === ruleId);

  // src/recipes/context.ts
  var isOidc = (e) => e.protocol === "OIDC";
  var isSaml = (e) => e.protocol === "SAML";
  var buildTraceContext = (session) => {
    const events = session?.normalizedEvents ?? [];
    const kzeroHosts = [...new Set(events.map((e) => e.host).filter((h) => h.endsWith("auth.kzero.com")))];
    const tenants = events.map((e) => e.url.match(/\/realms\/([^/]+)/i)?.[1]).filter((v) => Boolean(v));
    const oidc = events.filter(isOidc);
    const saml = events.filter(isSaml);
    return {
      kzeroHosts,
      tenants,
      oidc: {
        discovery: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "discovery"),
        authorize: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "authorize"),
        callback: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "callback"),
        token: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "token"),
        jwks: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "jwks"),
        logout: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "logout")
      },
      saml: {
        request: saml.find((e) => e.kind === "saml-request"),
        response: saml.find((e) => e.kind === "saml-response")
      }
    };
  };

  // src/mappings/fieldMappings.ts
  var map = {
    OIDC_REDIRECT_URI_MISMATCH: {
      kzeroFields: ["Redirect URL", "Client ID"],
      vendorFields: ["Redirect URI / Callback URL", "Client ID"]
    },
    OIDC_DISCOVERY_ISSUER_MISMATCH: {
      kzeroFields: ["Use discovery endpoint", "Discovery Endpoint", "Issuer"],
      vendorFields: ["Issuer URL"]
    },
    OIDC_INVALID_CLIENT: {
      kzeroFields: ["Client ID", "Client Secret", "Client authentication"],
      vendorFields: ["Client ID", "Client Secret", "Token Auth Method"]
    },
    OIDC_INVALID_SCOPE: {
      kzeroFields: ["Client ID"],
      vendorFields: ["Scope", "OIDC Application Permissions"]
    },

  // src/recipes/buildRecipe.ts
  var urlExactMatchNote = "Exact match matters: scheme, host, path, query (if used), and trailing slash.";
  var baseVerify = [
    "Start capture, run login once, stop capture.",
    "Confirm the finding no longer appears and the flow progresses past the failing step.",
    "Export sanitized trace + attach to ticket if escalation is needed."
  ];
  var buildFixRecipe = (finding, ctx) => {
    const map2 = getFieldMapping(finding.ruleId);
    const kzeroTenantHint = ctx.tenants[0] ? `Tenant: ${ctx.tenants[0]} (case-sensitive)` : "Tenant name is case-sensitive";
    switch (finding.ruleId) {
      case "OIDC_REDIRECT_URI_MISMATCH": {
        const expected = finding.expected;
        const observed = finding.observed;
        const clientId = ctx.oidc.authorize?.clientId ?? ctx.oidc.token?.clientId;

          title: "Redirect URI mismatch",
          owner: finding.likelyOwner,

            {
              title: "Fix in KZero",
              owner: "KZero",

              title: "Fix in vendor app (SP)",
              owner: "vendor SP",
              bullets: [
                `Update vendor "Redirect URI / Callback URL" to exactly: ${expected}`,
                urlExactMatchNote,
                "If the vendor supports multiple redirect URIs, remove stale ones from other environments.",
                "Retry login after saving changes."
              ],
              vendorFields: map2.vendorFields,
              copySnippets: [{ label: "Vendor Redirect URI", value: expected }]

              bullets: [`Requested redirect_uri: ${expected}`, `Browser callback reached: ${observed}`]
            }
          ],

            ...baseVerify,
            "In the new trace, ensure authorize request redirect_uri equals the callback URL that is reached."
          ],
          nextEvidence: ["Authorize request URL", "Callback URL", "Configured redirect/callback URI on vendor side"]
        };
      }
      case "OIDC_DISCOVERY_ISSUER_MISMATCH": {
        const discoveryUrl = ctx.oidc.discovery?.url;
        const issuerObserved = ctx.oidc.discovery?.issuer ?? finding.observed;
        const issuerExpected = finding.expected;

          title: "Discovery issuer mismatch",
          owner: finding.likelyOwner,
          confidence: finding.confidence,
          sections: [
            {
              title: "Fix in KZero",
              owner: "KZero",
              bullets: [
                `Confirm the tenant name is correct and case-sensitive. ${kzeroTenantHint}.`,
                `If "Use discovery endpoint" is enabled, set "Discovery Endpoint" to the correct tenant discovery URL.`,

                { field: "Issuer", expected: issuerExpected }

              copySnippets: discoveryUrl ? [{ label: "Discovery URL used", value: discoveryUrl }] : void 0

              title: "Fix in vendor app (SP)",
              owner: "vendor SP",

                "If vendor uses discovery, configure it to the same Discovery Endpoint you used in KZero."

              vendorFields: map2.vendorFields,
              copySnippets: [{ label: "Expected Issuer", value: issuerExpected }]

          ],

          nextEvidence: ["Token endpoint response error_description", "Client auth method configured on both sides"]
        };

          function getSourceInfoErrorAddendum(source) {

          function validateExplicitKey(element, parentType) {
            {
              if (!element._store || element._store.validated || element.key != null) {
                return;
              }
              element._store.validated = true;
              var currentComponentErrorInfo = getCurrentComponentErrorInfo(parentType);
              if (ownerHasKeyUseWarning[currentComponentErrorInfo]) {
                return;
              }
              ownerHasKeyUseWarning[currentComponentErrorInfo] = true;
              var childOwner = "";
              if (element && element._owner && element._owner !== ReactCurrentOwner$1.current) {
                childOwner = " It was passed a child from " + getComponentNameFromType(element._owner.type) + ".";

              setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(null);
            }
          }
          function validateChildKeys(node, parentType) {
            {
              if (typeof node !== "object") {
                return;
              }
              if (isArray(node)) {
                for (var i = 0; i < node.length; i++) {
                  var child = node[i];
                  if (isValidElement(child)) {
                    validateExplicitKey(child, parentType);
                  }
                }
              } else if (isValidElement(node)) {
                if (node._store) {
                  node._store.validated = true;
                }
              } else if (node) {
                var iteratorFn = getIteratorFn(node);
                if (typeof iteratorFn === "function") {
                  if (iteratorFn !== node.entries) {
                    var iterator = iteratorFn.call(node);
                    var step;
                    while (!(step = iterator.next()).done) {
                      if (isValidElement(step.value)) {
                        validateExplicitKey(step.value, parentType);
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
          function validatePropTypes(element) {
            {
              var type = element.type;
              if (type === null || type === void 0 || typeof type === "string") {
                return;
              }
              var propTypes;
              if (typeof type === "function") {
                propTypes = type.propTypes;
              } else if (typeof type === "object" && (type.$$typeof === REACT_FORWARD_REF_TYPE || // Note: Memo only checks outer props here.
              // Inner props are checked in the reconciler.
              type.$$typeof === REACT_MEMO_TYPE)) {
                propTypes = type.propTypes;
              } else {
                return;
              }

                var name = getComponentNameFromType(type);
                checkPropTypes(propTypes, element.props, "prop", name, element);

                propTypesMisspellWarningShown = true;

              }
            }

          function validateFragmentProps(fragment) {
            {
              var keys = Object.keys(fragment.props);
              for (var i = 0; i < keys.length; i++) {
                var key = keys[i];
                if (key !== "children" && key !== "key") {
                  setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(fragment);
                  error("Invalid prop `%s` supplied to `React.Fragment`. React.Fragment can only have `key` and `children` props.", key);

                error("Invalid attribute `ref` supplied to `React.Fragment`.");
                setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(null);
              }
            }
          }
          var didWarnAboutKeySpread = {};
          function jsxWithValidation(type, props, key, isStaticChildren, source, self) {
            {
              var validType = isValidElementType(type);
              if (!validType) {
                var info = "";
                if (type === void 0 || typeof type === "object" && type !== null && Object.keys(type).length === 0) {
                  info += " You likely forgot to export your component from the file it's defined in, or you might have mixed up default and named imports.";
                }
                var sourceInfo = getSourceInfoErrorAddendum(source);
                if (sourceInfo) {
                  info += sourceInfo;
                } else {
                  info += getDeclarationErrorAddendum();
                }
                var typeString;
                if (type === null) {
                  typeString = "null";
                } else if (isArray(type)) {
                  typeString = "array";
                } else if (type !== void 0 && type.$$typeof === REACT_ELEMENT_TYPE) {
                  typeString = "<" + (getComponentNameFromType(type.type) || "Unknown") + " />";
                  info = " Did you accidentally export a JSX literal instead of a component?";
                } else {
                  typeString = typeof type;
                }
                error("React.jsx: type is invalid -- expected a string (for built-in components) or a class/function (for composite components) but got: %s.%s", typeString, info);
              }
              var element = jsxDEV(type, props, key, source, self);
              if (element == null) {
                return element;
              }
              if (validType) {
                var children = props.children;
                if (children !== void 0) {
                  if (isStaticChildren) {
                    if (isArray(children)) {
                      for (var i = 0; i < children.length; i++) {
                        validateChildKeys(children[i], type);
                      }
                      if (Object.freeze) {
                        Object.freeze(children);
                      }
                    } else {
                      error("React.jsx: Static children should always be an array. You are likely explicitly calling React.jsxs or React.jsxDEV. Use the Babel transform instead.");
                    }
                  } else {
                    validateChildKeys(children, type);
                  }
                }
              }
              {
                if (hasOwnProperty.call(props, "key")) {
                  var componentName = getComponentNameFromType(type);
                  var keys = Object.keys(props).filter(function(k) {
                    return k !== "key";
                  });
                  var beforeExample = keys.length > 0 ? "{key: someKey, " + keys.join(": ..., ") + ": ...}" : "{key: someKey}";
                  if (!didWarnAboutKeySpread[componentName + beforeExample]) {
                    var afterExample = keys.length > 0 ? "{" + keys.join(": ..., ") + ": ...}" : "{}";
                    error('A props object containing a "key" prop is being spread into JSX:\n  let props = %s;\n  <%s {...props} />\nReact keys must be passed directly to JSX without using spread:\n  let props = %s;\n  <%s key={someKey} {...props} />', beforeExample, componentName, afterExample, componentName);
                    didWarnAboutKeySpread[componentName + beforeExample] = true;
                  }
                }
              }
              if (type === REACT_FRAGMENT_TYPE) {
                validateFragmentProps(element);
              } else {
                validatePropTypes(element);
              }
              return element;
            }
          }
          function jsxWithValidationStatic(type, props, key) {
            {
              return jsxWithValidation(type, props, key, true);
            }
          }
          function jsxWithValidationDynamic(type, props, key) {
            {
              return jsxWithValidation(type, props, key, false);
            }
          }
