  // src/shared/redaction.ts
  var SECRET_KEYS = [
    "client_secret",
    "refresh_token",
    "access_token",
    "id_token",
    "authorization",
    "authorization",
    "code",
    "samlresponse",
    "samlrequest",
    "assertion",
    "singedinfo",
    "private_key",
    "certificate",
    "keystore"
  ];
  var SECRET_PATTERNS = [
    /secret/i,
    /token/i,
    /password/i,
    /credential/i,
    /private/i,
    /authorization/i,
    /assertion/i,
    /certificate/i,
    /keystore/i
  ];
  var isSecretKey = (key) => {
    const lower = key.toLowerCase();
    if (SECRET_KEYS.includes(lower)) return true;
    if (SECRET_PATTERNS.some((p) => p.test(key))) return true;
    return false;
  };
  var mask = (value, left = 4, right = 3) => {
    if (value.length <= left + right) {
      return "***";
    }
    return `${value.slice(0, left)}...${value.slice(-right)}`;
  };
  var redactValue = (key, value) => {
    if (typeof value !== "string") return value;
    if (isSecretKey(key)) return mask(value);
    if (["nameid", "email", "sub"].includes(key.toLowerCase())) return mask(value, 2, 2);
    return value;
  };
  var redactRecord = (record) => {
    const out = {};
    for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(record)) {
      out[k] = redactValue(k, v);
    }
    return out;
  };

  // src/export/sanitizedExport.ts
  var sanitizeEvent = (event) => ({
    ...event,
    artifacts: redactRecord(event.artifacts)
  });
  var buildSanitizedExport = (session) => {
    if (!session) return null;
    return {
      generatedAt: (/* @__PURE__ */ new Date()).toISOString(),
      product: "KZero Passwordless SSO Tracer",
      notice: "Captured auth data stays local unless explicitly exported.",
      tabId: session.tabId,
      events: session.normalizedEvents.map(sanitizeEvent),
      findings: session.findings
    };
  };

  // src/export/harExport.ts
  var statusText = (code) => {
    if (code === 0) return "Aborted";
    if (code < 200) return "Informational";
    if (code < 300) return "Success";
    if (code < 400) return "Redirect";
    if (code < 500) return "Client Error";
    if (code < 600) return "Server Error";
    return "Unknown";
  };
  var entriesFromSession = (session) => {
    if (!session) return [];
    return session.rawEvents.map((raw) => {
      const reqHeaders = Object.entries(raw.requestHeaders ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value }));
      const resHeaders = Object.entries(raw.responseHeaders ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value }));
      const qs = Object.entries(raw.queryParams ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value }));
      return {
        startedDateTime: new Date(raw.timestamp).toISOString(),
        time: raw.timingMs ?? 0,
        request: {
          method: raw.method ?? "GET",
          url: raw.url,
          httpVersion: "HTTP/1.1",
          headers: reqHeaders,
          queryString: qs,
          postData: raw.postBody ? {
            mimeType: "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
            text: raw.postBody
          } : void 0
        },
        response: {
          status: raw.statusCode ?? 0,
          statusText: statusText(raw.statusCode ?? 0),
          httpVersion: "HTTP/1.1",
          headers: resHeaders,
          content: {
            size: raw.responseBody ? raw.responseBody.length : 0,
            mimeType: "text/html",
            text: raw.responseBody
          },
          redirectURL: raw.redirectUrl ?? ""
        },
        cache: {},
        timings: {
          send: 0,
          wait: raw.timingMs ?? 0,
          receive: 0
        }
      };
    });
  };
  var buildHarExport = (session) => {
    const har = {
      log: {
        version: "1.2",
        creator: {
          name: "KZero Passwordless SSO Tracer",
          version: chrome.runtime.getManifest().version
        },
        entries: entriesFromSession(session)
      }
    };
    return JSON.stringify(har, null, 2);
  };
  var downloadHar = (session) => {
    if (!session) return;
    const content = buildHarExport(session);
    const blob = new Blob([content], { type: "application/json" });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `kzero-trace-${session.tabId}-${Date.now()}.har`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  };

  // src/export/csvExport.ts
  var escape = (value) => {
    if (value.includes(",") || value.includes('"') || value.includes("\n")) {
      return `"${value.replace(/"/g, '""')}"`;
    }
    return value;
  };
  var escapeRow = (values) => values.map(escape).join(",");
  var buildFindingsCsv = (session) => {
    if (!session) return "";
    const lines = [];
    lines.push(escapeRow([
      "Rule ID",
      "Severity",
      "Owner",
      "Protocol",
      "Title",
      "Explanation",
      "Expected",
      "Observed",
      "Confidence",
      "Linked Event"
    ]));
    for (const f of session.findings) {
      lines.push(escapeRow([
        f.ruleId,
        f.severity,
        f.likelyOwner,
        f.protocol,
        f.title,
        f.explanation,
        f.expected ?? "",
        f.observed ?? "",
        String(f.confidence),
        f.eventId ?? ""
      ]));
    }
    return lines.join("\n");
  };
  var downloadFindingsCsv = (session) => {
    if (!session) return;
    const content = buildFindingsCsv(session);
    const blob = new Blob([content], { type: "text/csv" });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `kzero-findings-${session.tabId}-${Date.now()}.csv`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  };
  var buildSummaryCsv = (session) => {
    if (!session) return "";
    const lines = [];
    lines.push("KZero Passwordless SSO Tracer \u2014 Session Summary");
    lines.push("");
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Tab ID", String(session.tabId)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Started", session.startedAt ? new Date(session.startedAt).toISOString() : "-"]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Stopped", session.stoppedAt ? new Date(session.stoppedAt).toISOString() : "-"]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Duration (ms)", session.startedAt && session.stoppedAt ? String(session.stoppedAt - session.startedAt) : "-"]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Total Events", String(session.normalizedEvents.length)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Total Findings", String(session.findings.length)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Problems", String(session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "error").length)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Warnings", String(session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "warning").length)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Notices", String(session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "info").length)]));
    lines.push("");
    lines.push("Findings:");
    lines.push(buildFindingsCsv(session));
    return lines.join("\n");
  };
  var downloadSummaryCsv = (session) => {
    if (!session) return;
    const content = buildSummaryCsv(session);
    const blob = new Blob([content], { type: "text/csv" });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `kzero-summary-${session.tabId}-${Date.now()}.csv`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  };

  // src/export/shareableExport.ts
  var buildShareableTrace = (session) => {
    const export_ = buildSanitizedExport(session);
    if (!export_ || !session) return null;
    return {
      v: 1,
      tabId: session.tabId,
      startedAt: session.startedAt,
      stoppedAt: session.stoppedAt,
      events: export_.events,
      findings: export_.findings,
      summary: {
        eventCount: session.normalizedEvents.length,
        findingCount: session.findings.length,
        problemCount: session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "error").length,
        warningCount: session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "warning").length,
        protocolHints: [...new Set(session.normalizedEvents.map((e) => e.protocol))].filter((p) => p !== "unknown")
      }
    };
  };
  var utf8ToBase64 = (str) => {
    const bytes = new TextEncoder().encode(str);
    let binary = "";
    for (let i = 0; i < bytes.byteLength; i++) {
      binary += String.fromCharCode(bytes[i]);
    }
    return btoa(binary);
  };
  var encodeShareableTrace = (trace) => {
    const json = JSON.stringify(trace);
    return utf8ToBase64(json);
  };
  var buildShareableLink = (session) => {
    const trace = buildShareableTrace(session);
    if (!trace) return null;
    const encoded = encodeShareableTrace(trace);
    const viewerUrl = chrome.runtime.getURL("viewer.html");
    return `${viewerUrl}?trace=${encoded}`;
  };
  var downloadShareableTrace = (session) => {
    const trace = buildShareableTrace(session);
    if (!trace) return;
    const encoded = encodeShareableTrace(trace);
    const blob = new Blob([encoded], { type: "text/plain" });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `kzero-trace-shareable-${trace.tabId}-${Date.now()}.txt`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  };
  var copyShareableLink = async (session) => {
    const link = buildShareableLink(session);
    if (!link) return null;
    await navigator.clipboard.writeText(link);
    return link;
  };

  // src/shared/ruleCatalog.ts

  var getRuleDoc = (ruleId) => RULE_CATALOG.find((rule) => rule.ruleId === ruleId);

  // src/recipes/context.ts
  var isOidc = (e) => e.protocol === "OIDC";
  var isSaml = (e) => e.protocol === "SAML";
  var buildTraceContext = (session) => {
    const events = session?.normalizedEvents ?? [];
    const kzeroHosts = [...new Set(events.map((e) => e.host).filter((h) => h.endsWith("auth.kzero.com")))];
    const tenants = events.map((e) => e.url.match(/\/realms\/([^/]+)/i)?.[1]).filter((v) => Boolean(v));
    const oidc = events.filter(isOidc);
    const saml = events.filter(isSaml);
    return {
      kzeroHosts,
      tenants,
      oidc: {
        discovery: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "discovery"),
        authorize: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "authorize"),
        callback: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "callback"),
        token: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "token"),
        jwks: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "jwks"),
        logout: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "logout")
      },
      saml: {
        request: saml.find((e) => e.kind === "saml-request"),
        response: saml.find((e) => e.kind === "saml-response")
      }
    };
  };

  var getFieldMapping = (ruleId) => map[ruleId] ?? { kzeroFields: ["Alias", "Display name"], vendorFields: ["App config values"] };

  // src/recipes/buildRecipe.ts
  var urlExactMatchNote = "Exact match matters: scheme, host, path, query (if used), and trailing slash.";
  var baseVerify = [
    "Start capture, run login once, stop capture.",
    "Confirm the finding no longer appears and the flow progresses past the failing step.",
    "Export sanitized trace + attach to ticket if escalation is needed."
  ];
  var buildFixRecipe = (finding, ctx) => {
    const map2 = getFieldMapping(finding.ruleId);
    const kzeroTenantHint = ctx.tenants[0] ? `Tenant: ${ctx.tenants[0]} (case-sensitive)` : "Tenant name is case-sensitive";
    switch (finding.ruleId) {
      case "OIDC_REDIRECT_URI_MISMATCH": {
        const expected = finding.expected;
        const observed = finding.observed;
        const clientId = ctx.oidc.authorize?.clientId ?? ctx.oidc.token?.clientId;
        return {
          title: "Redirect URI mismatch",
          owner: finding.likelyOwner,
          confidence: finding.confidence,
          sections: [
            {
              title: "Fix in KZero",
              owner: "KZero",
              bullets: [
                `Open the OIDC Identity Provider config used for this integration (${kzeroTenantHint}).`,
                `Set "Redirect URL" to exactly the vendor callback URL. ${urlExactMatchNote}`,
                clientId ? `Confirm "Client ID" is ${clientId}.` : 'Confirm "Client ID" matches the vendor app.',
                'If "Use discovery endpoint" is enabled, confirm "Discovery Endpoint" points to the correct tenant.'
              ],
              kzeroFields: map2.kzeroFields,
              fieldExpectations: [{ field: "Redirect URL", expected }],
              copySnippets: [{ label: "Expected Redirect URL", value: expected }]
            },
            {
              title: "Fix in vendor app (SP)",
              owner: "vendor SP",
              bullets: [
                `Update vendor "Redirect URI / Callback URL" to exactly: ${expected}`,
                urlExactMatchNote,
                "If the vendor supports multiple redirect URIs, remove stale ones from other environments.",
                "Retry login after saving changes."
              ],
              vendorFields: map2.vendorFields,
              copySnippets: [{ label: "Vendor Redirect URI", value: expected }]
            },
            {
              title: "What we observed",
              owner: "browser",
              bullets: [`Requested redirect_uri: ${expected}`, `Browser callback reached: ${observed}`]
            }
          ],
          verify: [
            ...baseVerify,
            "In the new trace, ensure authorize request redirect_uri equals the callback URL that is reached."
          ],
          nextEvidence: ["Authorize request URL", "Callback URL", "Configured redirect/callback URI on vendor side"]
        };
      }
      case "OIDC_DISCOVERY_ISSUER_MISMATCH": {
        const discoveryUrl = ctx.oidc.discovery?.url;
        const issuerObserved = ctx.oidc.discovery?.issuer ?? finding.observed;
        const issuerExpected = finding.expected;
        return {
          title: "Discovery issuer mismatch",
          owner: finding.likelyOwner,
          confidence: finding.confidence,
          sections: [
            {
              title: "Fix in KZero",
              owner: "KZero",
              bullets: [
                `Confirm the tenant name is correct and case-sensitive. ${kzeroTenantHint}.`,
                `If "Use discovery endpoint" is enabled, set "Discovery Endpoint" to the correct tenant discovery URL.`,
                `Set "Issuer" to exactly: ${issuerExpected}`,
                "Avoid mixing values across environments (prod vs staging)."
              ],
              kzeroFields: map2.kzeroFields,
              fieldExpectations: [
                { field: "Discovery Endpoint", expected: ctx.oidc.discovery?.url ?? "" },
                { field: "Issuer", expected: issuerExpected }
              ].filter((e) => e.expected.length > 0),
              copySnippets: discoveryUrl ? [{ label: "Discovery URL used", value: discoveryUrl }] : void 0
            },
            {
              title: "Fix in vendor app (SP)",
              owner: "vendor SP",
              bullets: [
                `Set vendor "Issuer" / "Authority" to exactly: ${issuerExpected}`,
                "If vendor uses discovery, configure it to the same Discovery Endpoint you used in KZero."
              ],
              vendorFields: map2.vendorFields,
              copySnippets: [{ label: "Expected Issuer", value: issuerExpected }]
            },
            {
              title: "What we observed",
              owner: "browser",
              bullets: [
                discoveryUrl ? `Discovery URL: ${discoveryUrl}` : "Discovery URL captured",
                `issuer in discovery: ${issuerObserved}`,
                `expected issuer: ${issuerExpected}`
              ]
            }
          ],
          verify: [
            ...baseVerify,
            "In the new trace, discovery issuer equals the tenant base URL and matches token iss when JWT is present."
          ],
          nextEvidence: ["Discovery URL", "Discovery response issuer", "Tenant name and casing"]
        };
      }
      case "OIDC_INVALID_CLIENT": {
        const clientId = ctx.oidc.authorize?.clientId ?? ctx.oidc.token?.clientId;
        return {
          title: "Client authentication failed (invalid_client)",
          owner: finding.likelyOwner,
          confidence: finding.confidence,

            {
              title: "Fix in KZero",
              owner: "KZero",

                'Re-enter "Client Secret" (do not paste leading/trailing spaces).',
                'Verify "Client authentication" matches what the vendor uses (basic vs post vs private_key_jwt).'
              ],
              kzeroFields: map2.kzeroFields
            },
            {
              title: "Fix in vendor app (SP)",

  const existing = sessions.get(tabId);
  if (existing) return existing;
  const stored = sessionCache.get(tabId);
  if (stored) {
    sessions.set(tabId, stored);
    return stored;
  }
  const created: CaptureSession = {    tabId,
    active: false,
    rawEvents: [],
    normalizedEvents: [],
    findings: []
  };
  sessions.set(tabId, created);  return created;
};
```If `tabId` is -1, it will create a new session for tabId=-1. This is fine - it will have `normalizedEvents: []` which is not null.So `getSession` should never return null.           function getSourceInfoErrorAddendum(source) {

          function validateExplicitKey(element, parentType) {
            {
              if (!element._store || element._store.validated || element.key != null) {
                return;
              }
              element._store.validated = true;
              var currentComponentErrorInfo = getCurrentComponentErrorInfo(parentType);
              if (ownerHasKeyUseWarning[currentComponentErrorInfo]) {
                return;
              }
              ownerHasKeyUseWarning[currentComponentErrorInfo] = true;
              var childOwner = "";
              if (element && element._owner && element._owner !== ReactCurrentOwner$1.current) {

              setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(null);
            }
          }
          function validateChildKeys(node, parentType) {
            {
              if (typeof node !== "object") {
                return;
              }
              if (isArray(node)) {
                for (var i = 0; i < node.length; i++) {
                  var child = node[i];
                  if (isValidElement(child)) {
                    validateExplicitKey(child, parentType);
                  }
                }
              } else if (isValidElement(node)) {
                if (node._store) {
                  node._store.validated = true;
                }
              } else if (node) {
                var iteratorFn = getIteratorFn(node);
                if (typeof iteratorFn === "function") {
                  if (iteratorFn !== node.entries) {
                    var iterator = iteratorFn.call(node);
                    var step;
                    while (!(step = iterator.next()).done) {
                      if (isValidElement(step.value)) {
                        validateExplicitKey(step.value, parentType);
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
          function validatePropTypes(element) {
            {
              var type = element.type;
              if (type === null || type === void 0 || typeof type === "string") {
                return;
              }
              var propTypes;
              if (typeof type === "function") {
                propTypes = type.propTypes;
              } else if (typeof type === "object" && (type.$$typeof === REACT_FORWARD_REF_TYPE || // Note: Memo only checks outer props here.
              // Inner props are checked in the reconciler.
              type.$$typeof === REACT_MEMO_TYPE)) {
                propTypes = type.propTypes;
              } else {
                return;
              }
              if (propTypes) {
                var name = getComponentNameFromType(type);
                checkPropTypes(propTypes, element.props, "prop", name, element);
              } else if (type.PropTypes !== void 0 && !propTypesMisspellWarningShown) {
                propTypesMisspellWarningShown = true;
                var _name = getComponentNameFromType(type);

              }
            }
          }
          function validateFragmentProps(fragment) {
            {
              var keys = Object.keys(fragment.props);
              for (var i = 0; i < keys.length; i++) {
                var key = keys[i];
                if (key !== "children" && key !== "key") {
                  setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(fragment);
                  error("Invalid prop `%s` supplied to `React.Fragment`. React.Fragment can only have `key` and `children` props.", key);

                setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(fragment);
                error("Invalid attribute `ref` supplied to `React.Fragment`.");
                setCurrentlyValidatingElement$1(null);
              }
            }
          }
          var didWarnAboutKeySpread = {};
          function jsxWithValidation(type, props, key, isStaticChildren, source, self) {
            {
              var validType = isValidElementType(type);
              if (!validType) {
                var info = "";
                if (type === void 0 || typeof type === "object" && type !== null && Object.keys(type).length === 0) {
                  info += " You likely forgot to export your component from the file it's defined in, or you might have mixed up default and named imports.";
                }
                var sourceInfo = getSourceInfoErrorAddendum(source);
                if (sourceInfo) {
                  info += sourceInfo;
                } else {
                  info += getDeclarationErrorAddendum();
                }
                var typeString;
                if (type === null) {
                  typeString = "null";
                } else if (isArray(type)) {
                  typeString = "array";
                } else if (type !== void 0 && type.$$typeof === REACT_ELEMENT_TYPE) {
                  typeString = "<" + (getComponentNameFromType(type.type) || "Unknown") + " />";
                  info = " Did you accidentally export a JSX literal instead of a component?";
                } else {
                  typeString = typeof type;
                }
                error("React.jsx: type is invalid -- expected a string (for built-in components) or a class/function (for composite components) but got: %s.%s", typeString, info);
              }
              var element = jsxDEV(type, props, key, source, self);
              if (element == null) {
                return element;
              }
              if (validType) {
                var children = props.children;
                if (children !== void 0) {
                  if (isStaticChildren) {
                    if (isArray(children)) {
                      for (var i = 0; i < children.length; i++) {
                        validateChildKeys(children[i], type);
                      }
                      if (Object.freeze) {
                        Object.freeze(children);
                      }
                    } else {
                      error("React.jsx: Static children should always be an array. You are likely explicitly calling React.jsxs or React.jsxDEV. Use the Babel transform instead.");
                    }
                  } else {
                    validateChildKeys(children, type);
                  }
                }
              }
              {
                if (hasOwnProperty.call(props, "key")) {
                  var componentName = getComponentNameFromType(type);
                  var keys = Object.keys(props).filter(function(k) {
                    return k !== "key";
                  });
                  var beforeExample = keys.length > 0 ? "{key: someKey, " + keys.join(": ..., ") + ": ...}" : "{key: someKey}";
                  if (!didWarnAboutKeySpread[componentName + beforeExample]) {
                    var afterExample = keys.length > 0 ? "{" + keys.join(": ..., ") + ": ...}" : "{}";
                    error('A props object containing a "key" prop is being spread into JSX:\n  let props = %s;\n  <%s {...props} />\nReact keys must be passed directly to JSX without using spread:\n  let props = %s;\n  <%s key={someKey} {...props} />', beforeExample, componentName, afterExample, componentName);
                    didWarnAboutKeySpread[componentName + beforeExample] = true;
                  }
                }
              }
              if (type === REACT_FRAGMENT_TYPE) {
                validateFragmentProps(element);
              } else {
                validatePropTypes(element);
              }
              return element;
            }
          }
          function jsxWithValidationStatic(type, props, key) {
            {
              return jsxWithValidation(type, props, key, true);
            }
          }
          function jsxWithValidationDynamic(type, props, key) {
            {
              return jsxWithValidation(type, props, key, false);
            }
          }

      this.setState({ hasError: false, error: null });

  // src/shared/redaction.ts
  var SECRET_KEYS = [
    "client_secret",
    "refresh_token",
    "access_token",
    "id_token",
    "authorization",
    "authorization",
    "code",
    "samlresponse",
    "samlrequest",
    "assertion",
    "singedinfo",
    "private_key",
    "certificate",
    "keystore"
  ];
  var SECRET_PATTERNS = [
    /secret/i,
    /token/i,
    /password/i,
    /credential/i,
    /private/i,
    /authorization/i,
    /assertion/i,
    /certificate/i,
    /keystore/i
  ];
  var isSecretKey = (key) => {
    const lower = key.toLowerCase();
    if (SECRET_KEYS.includes(lower)) return true;
    if (SECRET_PATTERNS.some((p) => p.test(key))) return true;
    return false;
  };
  var mask = (value, left = 4, right = 3) => {
    if (value.length <= left + right) {
      return "***";
    }
    return `${value.slice(0, left)}...${value.slice(-right)}`;
  };
  var redactValue = (key, value) => {
    if (typeof value !== "string") return value;
    if (isSecretKey(key)) return mask(value);
    if (["nameid", "email", "sub"].includes(key.toLowerCase())) return mask(value, 2, 2);
    return value;
  };
  var redactRecord = (record) => {
    const out = {};
    for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(record)) {
      out[k] = redactValue(k, v);
    }
    return out;
  };

  // src/export/sanitizedExport.ts
  var sanitizeEvent = (event) => ({
    ...event,
    artifacts: redactRecord(event.artifacts)
  });
  var buildSanitizedExport = (session) => {
    if (!session) return null;
    return {
      generatedAt: (/* @__PURE__ */ new Date()).toISOString(),
      product: "KZero Passwordless SSO Tracer",
      notice: "Captured auth data stays local unless explicitly exported.",
      tabId: session.tabId,
      events: session.normalizedEvents.map(sanitizeEvent),
      findings: session.findings
    };
  };

  // src/export/harExport.ts
  var statusText = (code) => {
    if (code === 0) return "Aborted";
    if (code < 200) return "Informational";
    if (code < 300) return "Success";
    if (code < 400) return "Redirect";
    if (code < 500) return "Client Error";
    if (code < 600) return "Server Error";
    return "Unknown";
  };
  var entriesFromSession = (session) => {
    if (!session) return [];
    return session.rawEvents.map((raw) => {
      const reqHeaders = Object.entries(raw.requestHeaders ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value }));
      const resHeaders = Object.entries(raw.responseHeaders ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value }));
      const qs = Object.entries(raw.queryParams ?? {}).map(([name, value]) => ({ name, value }));
      return {
        startedDateTime: new Date(raw.timestamp).toISOString(),
        time: raw.timingMs ?? 0,
        request: {
          method: raw.method ?? "GET",
          url: raw.url,
          httpVersion: "HTTP/1.1",
          headers: reqHeaders,
          queryString: qs,
          postData: raw.postBody ? {
            mimeType: "application/x-www-form-urlencoded",
            text: raw.postBody
          } : void 0
        },
        response: {
          status: raw.statusCode ?? 0,
          statusText: statusText(raw.statusCode ?? 0),
          httpVersion: "HTTP/1.1",
          headers: resHeaders,
          content: {
            size: raw.responseBody ? raw.responseBody.length : 0,
            mimeType: "text/html",
            text: raw.responseBody
          },
          redirectURL: raw.redirectUrl ?? ""
        },
        cache: {},
        timings: {
          send: 0,
          wait: raw.timingMs ?? 0,
          receive: 0
        }
      };
    });
  };
  var buildHarExport = (session) => {
    const har = {
      log: {
        version: "1.2",
        creator: {
          name: "KZero Passwordless SSO Tracer",
          version: chrome.runtime.getManifest().version
        },
        entries: entriesFromSession(session)
      }
    };
    return JSON.stringify(har, null, 2);
  };
  var downloadHar = (session) => {
    if (!session) return;
    const content = buildHarExport(session);
    const blob = new Blob([content], { type: "application/json" });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `kzero-trace-${session.tabId}-${Date.now()}.har`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  };

  // src/export/csvExport.ts
  var escape = (value) => {
    if (value.includes(",") || value.includes('"') || value.includes("\n")) {
      return `"${value.replace(/"/g, '""')}"`;
    }
    return value;
  };
  var escapeRow = (values) => values.map(escape).join(",");
  var buildFindingsCsv = (session) => {
    if (!session) return "";
    const lines = [];
    lines.push(escapeRow([
      "Rule ID",
      "Severity",
      "Owner",
      "Protocol",
      "Title",
      "Explanation",
      "Expected",
      "Observed",
      "Confidence",
      "Linked Event"
    ]));
    for (const f of session.findings) {
      lines.push(escapeRow([
        f.ruleId,
        f.severity,
        f.likelyOwner,
        f.protocol,
        f.title,
        f.explanation,
        f.expected ?? "",
        f.observed ?? "",
        String(f.confidence),
        f.eventId ?? ""
      ]));
    }
    return lines.join("\n");
  };
  var downloadFindingsCsv = (session) => {
    if (!session) return;
    const content = buildFindingsCsv(session);
    const blob = new Blob([content], { type: "text/csv" });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `kzero-findings-${session.tabId}-${Date.now()}.csv`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  };
  var buildSummaryCsv = (session) => {
    if (!session) return "";
    const lines = [];
    lines.push("KZero Passwordless SSO Tracer \u2014 Session Summary");
    lines.push("");
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Tab ID", String(session.tabId)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Started", session.startedAt ? new Date(session.startedAt).toISOString() : "-"]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Stopped", session.stoppedAt ? new Date(session.stoppedAt).toISOString() : "-"]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Duration (ms)", session.startedAt && session.stoppedAt ? String(session.stoppedAt - session.startedAt) : "-"]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Total Events", String(session.normalizedEvents.length)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Total Findings", String(session.findings.length)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Problems", String(session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "error").length)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Warnings", String(session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "warning").length)]));
    lines.push(escapeRow(["Notices", String(session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "info").length)]));
    lines.push("");
    lines.push("Findings:");
    lines.push(buildFindingsCsv(session));
    return lines.join("\n");
  };
  var downloadSummaryCsv = (session) => {
    if (!session) return;
    const content = buildSummaryCsv(session);
    const blob = new Blob([content], { type: "text/csv" });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `kzero-summary-${session.tabId}-${Date.now()}.csv`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  };

  // src/export/shareableExport.ts
  var buildShareableTrace = (session) => {
    const export_ = buildSanitizedExport(session);
    if (!export_ || !session) return null;
    return {
      v: 1,
      tabId: session.tabId,
      startedAt: session.startedAt,
      stoppedAt: session.stoppedAt,
      events: export_.events,
      findings: export_.findings,
      summary: {
        eventCount: session.normalizedEvents.length,
        findingCount: session.findings.length,
        problemCount: session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "error").length,
        warningCount: session.findings.filter((f) => f.severity === "warning").length,
        protocolHints: [...new Set(session.normalizedEvents.map((e) => e.protocol))].filter((p) => p !== "unknown")
      }
    };
  };
  var utf8ToBase64 = (str) => {
    const bytes = new TextEncoder().encode(str);
    let binary = "";
    for (let i = 0; i < bytes.byteLength; i++) {
      binary += String.fromCharCode(bytes[i]);
    }
    return btoa(binary);
  };
  var encodeShareableTrace = (trace) => {
    const json = JSON.stringify(trace);
    return utf8ToBase64(json);
  };
  var buildShareableLink = (session) => {
    const trace = buildShareableTrace(session);
    if (!trace) return null;
    const encoded = encodeShareableTrace(trace);
    const viewerUrl = chrome.runtime.getURL("viewer.html");
    return `${viewerUrl}?trace=${encoded}`;
  };
  var downloadShareableTrace = (session) => {
    const trace = buildShareableTrace(session);
    if (!trace) return;
    const encoded = encodeShareableTrace(trace);
    const blob = new Blob([encoded], { type: "text/plain" });
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
    const a = document.createElement("a");
    a.href = url;
    a.download = `kzero-trace-shareable-${trace.tabId}-${Date.now()}.txt`;
    a.click();
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
  };
  var copyShareableLink = async (session) => {
    const link = buildShareableLink(session);
    if (!link) return null;
    await navigator.clipboard.writeText(link);
    return link;
  };

  // src/recipes/context.ts
  var isOidc = (e) => e.protocol === "OIDC";
  var isSaml = (e) => e.protocol === "SAML";
  var buildTraceContext = (session) => {
    const events = session?.normalizedEvents ?? [];
    const kzeroHosts = [...new Set(events.map((e) => e.host).filter((h) => h.endsWith("auth.kzero.com")))];
    const tenants = events.map((e) => e.url.match(/\/realms\/([^/]+)/i)?.[1]).filter((v) => Boolean(v));
    const oidc = events.filter(isOidc);
    const saml = events.filter(isSaml);
    return {
      kzeroHosts,
      tenants,
      oidc: {
        discovery: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "discovery"),
        authorize: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "authorize"),
        callback: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "callback"),
        token: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "token"),
        jwks: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "jwks"),
        logout: oidc.find((e) => e.kind === "logout")
      },
      saml: {
        request: saml.find((e) => e.kind === "saml-request"),
        response: saml.find((e) => e.kind === "saml-response")
      }
    };
  };

**On success metrics:**
6. **Confidence after fix** - Should we add a "Did this help?" feedback button to track which fixes are most effective?

## Issue 1: No Automated Validation

- Con: Might be slow if many fields need scanning

- Pro: Gives immediate feedback before user reads steps
- Con: Still requires being on the right page

- Pro: Helps verify fixes without repeated manual checks
- Con: More complex, potential performance issues

**Option D: Browser MCP integration**
- Use browser MCP to automatically read KZero config values
- Use browser MCP to read vendor admin page values
- Pro: Fully automated comparison
- Con: Requires browser MCP to be configured

- Show side-by-side comparison with alignment
- Add visual diff highlighting in the check table

- Add visual representations of the SAML/OIDC structure to help users understand the context

--- Part 4043 ---
Based on my analysis, here's how we can fix these two issues:

---

## Issue 1: No Automated Validation

### Current Problem
The "Check fields" button requires manual user action. The `uiScan` results are only populated when user clicks "Check fields".

### Proposed Solutions

**Option A: Auto-scan on finding selection (Recommended)**
- Automatically trigger the field scan when a user selects a finding
- Fire the `scanFields()` request immediately when `selectedFindingId` changes
- Show a loading state while scanning
- Pro: Zero extra clicks, immediate feedback
- Con: Slight delay on selection

**Implementation:**
```typescript
// New useEffect that auto-triggers scan when finding changes
useEffect(() => {
  if (selectedFinding && mode === "devtools" && tabId >= 0) {
    const labels = getLabelsForFinding(selectedFinding);
    if (labels.length > 0) {
      requestUiScan(labels);
    }
  }
}, [selectedFindingId]);
```

**Option B: Add "Verification Mode" toggle**
- New state: `isVerificationMode`
- When enabled, periodically re-scans fields and shows real-time updates
- Visual indicator when values change
- Pro: Helps verify fixes in real-time
- Con: More complex, potential performance overhead

**Option C: Browser MCP Integration**
- Use browser MCP to automatically read config values from both KZero admin and vendor admin pages
- Would need MCP to be configured with access to both systems
- Pro: Fully automated
- Con: Requires additional setup by user

---

## Issue 2: Limited Visual Guidance

### Current Problem
- DiffLine shows observed vs expected with character-level highlighting but uses plain text
- No visual indicators for match/mismatch status (just "bad" class for red text)
- PreFlightChecklist is static with no status tracking

### Proposed Solutions

**Option A: Enhanced Diff Display with Status Icons**

Current table:
| Field | Expected | Current | Status |
|-------|----------|---------|--------|
| Redirect URL | `https://...` | `https://...` | (not clear) |

Enhanced:
| Field | Expected | Current | Status |
|-------|----------|---------|--------|
| Redirect URL | `https://...` | `https://...` | ✅ Match |

**Option B: Progress Checklist with Real-time Status**

Current PreFlightChecklist:
```
✅ Redirect URL (KZero)
✅ Client ID (KZero)
✅ Redirect URI (Vendor)
```

Enhanced with auto-scan results:
```
✅ Redirect URL (KZero) — matches
❌ Redirect URI (Vendor) — mismatch
⏳ ACS URL (Vendor) — not found
```

**Option C: Visual Diff Summary Card**

Add a summary card at the top of the Fix tab showing:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  🔴 1 mismatch found                          │
│                                                 │
│  Redirect URI                                   │
│  Expected: https://app.vendor.com/callback     │
│  Current:  https://app.vendor.com/callback/    │
│                              ↑ trailing slash    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Option D: Interactive Field Mapping Visualization**

Show a side-by-side diagram:
```
KZero Admin                    Vendor Admin
┌──────────────────┐          ┌──────────────────┐
│ Redirect URL      │          │ Callback URL     │
│ ┌──────────────┐ │    ≠    │ ┌──────────────┐ │
│ │ https://...   │ │ ──────▶ │ │ https://.../ │ │
│ └──────────────┘ │          │ └──────────────┘ │
└──────────────────┘          └──────────────────┘
```

---

## My Recommendation

**For Issue 1:** Implement **Option A (Auto-scan)** - it's the highest impact with lowest complexity. Automatically trigger the field scan when a user selects a finding.

**For Issue 2:** Implement **Options B + C**:
- **B**: Enhance PreFlightChecklist to show scan results with status indicators (✅/❌/⏳)
- **C**: Add a diff summary card that clearly shows the mismatch at a glance

```javascript

port.postMessage({ type: "PANEL_INIT", tabId: messagingTabId });
```

And in the background:
```javascript
chrome.runtime.onConnect.addListener((port) => {
  if (port.name !== "kzero-panel") return;
  let tabId = port.sender?.tab?.id ?? -1;
  port.onMessage.addListener((msg) => {
    if (msg.type === "PANEL_INIT") {
      tabId = msg.tabId ?? tabId;
      const ports = panelPorts.get(tabId) ?? [];
      ports.push(port);
      panelPorts.set(tabId, ports);
      port.postMessage({ type: "SESSION_UPDATE", session: getSession(tabId) });
    }
  });
```

The background receives the `PANEL_INIT` message with the tabId from the panel, but it's also trying to get the tabId from `port.sender?.tab?.id`. The problem is that `port.sender?.tab?.id` might be undefined or the wrong tabId, and the fallback `tabId = msg.tabId ?? tabId` might not be setting it correctly. Then when `START_CAPTURE` is sent with a different tabId, the broadcast happens to a different key in the `panelPorts` map, so the panel never receives the update.

I suspect the problem lies in how `startCapture` is being called. The handler references `startCapture` and `stopCapture` directly, but these functions rely on `messagingTabId`, which might be undefined at the time the effect runs. Even though `startCapture` is defined before the effect that sets up the message listener, the dependency array only includes `messagingTabId`, so the handler might be capturing stale references or the tab ID might not be properly initialized when the handler executes. The issue is that `stopCapture` and `startCapture` are being referenced inside the effect before they're defined. In React, function declarations are hoisted so this works, but closures capture the current `session` state. When `stopCapture` is called, it references `session` from the closure, but there's a race condition—`setSession` is asynchronous, so if the effect fires multiple times rapidly, the session state might not reflect the latest value when `stopCapture` executes. The callback captures `session` at the time the effect runs, which should work correctly. When `toggle-capture` is invoked, the handler checks the current `session?.active` state and calls either `stopCapture()` or `startCapture()` accordingly.

Checking the recent history reveals several file modifications across the background, content scripts, and panel components, plus some infrastructure updates. I need to trace through what actually changed in the capture session logic and message handling to identify where the regression occurred.

The manifest updates are worth investigating—specifically the addition of scripting permissions, the removal of content script declarations, and the inclusion of a viewer.html file. These modifications to how the extension loads and what it can execute could be affecting the tracing behavior, especially if the content script injection approach changed.

--- Part 4412 ---
I found it! The `content_scripts` section was removed from the manifest:```diff
-  "content_scripts": [  -    {
-      "matches": ["<all_urls>"],
-      "js": ["content.js"],
-      "run_at": "document_idle"  -    }
-  ],+  "content_scripts": [],
```

This means the content script is no longer automatically injected into all pages! The content script was what captured form submissions (SAML forms). Without automatic injection, users would need to manually inject the content script or it would need to be injected via other means.

Looking at the background code, there's a `REQUEST_UI_SCAN` handler that tries to inject the content script:```javascriptif (message.type === "REQUEST_UI_SCAN") {  void (async () => {    try {      await chrome.scripting.executeScript({        target: { tabId: message.tabId },        files: ["content.js"]      });      ...    } catch (err) {      respond({ ok: false, error: `Could not inject content script: ${err}` });    }  })();}```

But this is only called when `REQUEST_UI_SCAN` is sent, which is for UI scanning (checking field values). The form capture happens when the content script loads and adds event listeners to forms.The content script also scans forms on page load and uses MutationObserver to detect new forms. If the content script isn't loaded, none of this happens.The fix is to either:
1. Restore the content_scripts in the manifest2. Or ensure the content script is injected some other way (e.g., via declarativeContent)

Let me check what the original content_scripts configuration was and restore it.

--- Part 4413 ---

Found the issue! The manifest had `content_scripts` removed that auto-inject the content script. This is needed for capturing SAML form submissions. Let me restore it:

--- Part 4417 ---
Good, I restored the content_scripts configuration. Now let me rebuild to verify everything works.

--- Part 4421 ---

This broke SAML form capture since the content script is responsible for detecting and capturing SAML form submissions. Restored the original content_scripts configuration.

---

# Plan: Beginner-Friendly SSO Troubleshooting Overhaul

## Goal
Transform the tracer's fix guidance from technical jargon into step-by-step instructions a level 1 technician can follow, with clear navigation paths and plain-English explanations.

---

## Scope Summary

| Category | Count | Status |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Total Rules | 37 | - |
| Has dedicated recipes | ~12 | ✅ Existing |
| Needs new recipes | ~15 | ❌ Missing |
| Needs improved guidance | ~10 | ⚠️ Vague |

---

## Phase 1: Add Missing Recipes (~15 rules)

### 1.1 SAML Rules Without Guidance
| Rule ID | Finding | Why It Fails |
|---------|---------|--------------|
| `SAML_UNPARSEABLE_ARTIFACT` | Artifact decode failed | Base64 corruption, truncation |
| `SAML_RELAYSTATE_UNEXPECTED` | RelayState mismatch | SP expects different flow |
| `SAML_INRESPONSETO_MISMATCH` | InResponseTo missing | SP-initiated flow expected |
| `SAML_ASSERTION_ENCRYPTED` | Encrypted assertion | Vendor can't decrypt |
| `SAML_WRONG_BINDING_CLUE` | Wrong response binding | SP expects POST, got redirect |
| `SAML_ASSERTION_SIGNATURE_MISSING` | No assertion signature | "Sign assertions" disabled |
| `SAML_DOCUMENT_SIGNATURE_MISSING` | No document signature | "Sign documents" disabled |

**Key additions:**
- Explain what IdP-initiated SSO means and KZero's limitations
- Guide user to check "Force POST binding" vs redirect binding
- Explain signing requirements for each party

### 1.2 OIDC Rules Without Guidance
| Rule ID | Finding |
|---------|---------|
| `OIDC_DISCOVERY_UNREACHABLE` | Discovery URL not accessible |
| `OIDC_NONCE_MISSING` | No nonce in auth request |
| `OIDC_STATE_MISSING_OR_MISMATCH` | CSRF state issue |
| `OIDC_TOKEN_ISSUER_MISMATCH` | Token issuer ≠ discovery |
| `OIDC_UNAUTHORIZED_CLIENT` | Client not permitted |
| `OIDC_UNSUPPORTED_RESPONSE_TYPE` | Wrong response_type |
| `OIDC_UNSUPPORTED_RESPONSE_MODE` | Wrong response_mode |

### 1.3 Cross-Protocol Rules
| Rule ID | Finding |
|---------|---------|
| `WRONG_HOST_OR_ENVIRONMENT` | Mixed prod/staging URLs |
| `WRONG_REALM_ENDPOINT_FAMILY` | Legacy `/auth/` path used |
| `NETWORK_TLS_REACHABILITY_SUSPECTED` | Firewall/WAF blocking |
| `STALE_VALUES_FROM_ANOTHER_ENVIRONMENT` | Copy/paste from wrong env |

---

## Phase 2: Improve Existing Recipes

### 2.1 Add Step-by-Step UI Navigation

**Current:** "Set Assertion Consumer Service URL to X"

**Enhanced:** 
```
Step 1: Go to your KZero admin panel
   → Navigate to: Clients → [Select Client] → Settings tab
   → Scroll down to "Access settings" section

Step 2: Find the "Master SAML Processing URL" field
   → This is where you set the ACS URL

Step 3: Enter the exact URL: https://vendor.example.com/saml/acs
   → URL must match exactly (including https:// and trailing slash)

Step 4: Click "Save" at the bottom of the page
```

### 2.2 Add Plain-English Explanations

For each field, add:
- **What it is:** One-sentence explanation
- **Why it matters:** What breaks if wrong
- **Common mistakes:** What to watch for

**Example - ACS URL:**
```
ACS URL (Assertion Consumer Service URL)
• What it is: The web address where the vendor app receives the login response from KZero
• Why it matters: If this is wrong, the vendor app never gets confirmation that you logged in
• Common mistakes: 
  - Forgetting the trailing slash (https://vendor.com/ ≠ https://vendor.com)
  - Using http:// instead of https://
  - Copying the wrong URL from vendor documentation
```

### 2.3 Add KZero Limitation Warnings

**Example for IdP-initiated SSO:**
```
⚠️ IMPORTANT: KZero SSO Limitation
Not all SSO flows are supported:

✅ SP-Initiated SSO: User goes to vendor app → redirected to KZero → login → return to vendor
✅ OIDC Authorization Code Flow: Modern, recommended flow

❌ Pure IdP-Initiated SSO: KZero does not support initiating login from the KZero side
   → If vendor requires IdP-initiated, you may need a different configuration

If your vendor requires IdP-initiated SSO, contact KZero support for alternatives.
```

---

## Phase 3: Add Feature Support Metadata

### 3.1 SSO Flow Support Matrix

Add to context/recipes:
```
SSO Flow Support:
| Flow | SAML | OIDC |
|------|------|------|
| SP-Initiated (redirect) | ✅ | ✅ |
| SP-Initiated (POST) | ✅ | N/A |
| IdP-Initiated | ⚠️ Limited | ⚠️ Limited |
| OIDC Hybrid Flow | N/A | ⚠️ Limited |
```

### 3.2 Certificate Requirements

Add guidance for:
- Certificate format (PEM vs DER vs Base64)
- How to export from KZero
- How to import to vendor
- Certificate fingerprint verification

---

## Phase 4: UI Improvements (Optional)

### 4.1 PreFlightChecklist Enhancements
- Show checkmarks for completed steps
- Add "Copy value" button for each field
- Show which vendor fields map to which KZero fields

### 4.2 DiffSummaryCard Improvements
- Clickable items that highlight the field on page
- "How to fix" tooltips

## Implementation Structure

```
src/recipes/
  ├── buildRecipe.ts          # Main recipe builder (MODIFY)
  ├── context.ts              # Trace context (MODIFY - add support metadata)
  ├── types.ts                # Recipe types (MODIFY - add navigation hints)
  └── guidance/
      ├── samlGuidance.ts     # NEW - SAML-specific guidance templates
      ├── oidcGuidance.ts     # NEW - OIDC-specific guidance templates  
      ├── kzeroNavigation.ts  # NEW - KZero admin UI navigation paths
      └── plainEnglish.ts     # NEW - Field explanations in plain language
```

# FINAL PLAN: Beginner-Friendly SSO Troubleshooting for KZero

## Overview

Transform the tracer's fix guidance from technical jargon into actionable, step-by-step instructions that level 1 technicians can follow, with:
- **Plain-English explanations** of what each field means and why it matters
- **KZero admin UI navigation** (step-by-step paths to find each setting)
- **Documentation links** to your existing guides at docs.kzero.com
- **"What went wrong"** explanations in non-technical language
- **Known limitations** clearly documented (if any)

---

## Implementation Strategy

### 1. New File Structure

```
src/recipes/
  ├── buildRecipe.ts           # Main recipe builder (MODIFY)
  ├── guidance/
  │   ├── index.ts             # NEW: Central guidance hub
  │   ├── navigation.ts        # NEW: KZero admin UI navigation paths  
  │   ├── saml.ts              # NEW: SAML-specific guidance & explanations
  │   ├── oidc.ts              # NEW: OIDC-specific guidance & explanations
  │   └── terminology.ts        # NEW: Plain-English glossary
  └── types.ts                 # MODIFY: Add navigation hint support
```

---

### 2. Key Additions

#### A. Plain-English Glossary (`terminology.ts`)
Non-technical explanations for common terms:

```typescript
export const terminology = {
  "ACS URL": {
    what: "Where the login response gets delivered",
    why: "The vendor app won't know you logged in if this is wrong",
    plain: "Think of it like a delivery address - the login confirmation gets sent here"
  },
  "Entity ID": {
    what: "A unique name for each party in the SSO handshake",
    why: "Both sides need to agree on who each other are",
    plain: "Like a company name on a business card"
  },
  // ... etc
}
```

#### B. KZero Navigation Paths (`navigation.ts`)
Step-by-step paths to each setting in your admin UI:

```typescript
export const kzeroNavigation = {
  "SAML Client Settings": {
    path: "Clients → [Select Client] → Settings tab",
    sections: ["General settings", "Access settings", "SAML capabilities", "Signature and Encryption", "Login settings", "Logout settings"],
    fields: {
      "ACS URL": "Access settings → Master SAML Processing URL",
      "Entity ID": "General settings → Client ID",
      // ... etc
    }
  },
  "OIDC Client Settings": {
    path: "Clients → [Select Client] → Settings tab",
    sections: ["General settings", "Access settings", "Capability config", "Login settings", "Logout settings"],
    fields: {
      "Redirect URL": "Access settings → Valid redirect URIs",
      "Client ID": "General settings → Client ID",
      // ... etc
    }
  },
  "Realm Endpoints": {
    path: "Realm settings → General tab → Endpoints section",
    links: {
      "OIDC Discovery": "OpenID Endpoint Configuration",
      "SAML Metadata": "SAML 2.0 Identity Provider Metadata"
    }
  }
}
```

#### C. SSO Flow Support Matrix (`index.ts`)
Clear documentation of what's supported:

```typescript
export const flowSupport = {
  saml: {
    "SP-Initiated (Redirect)": { supported: true },
    "SP-Initiated (POST)": { supported: true },
    "IdP-Initiated": { supported: true, note: "Requires IDP-Initiated SSO URL configured" }
  },
  oidc: {
    "Authorization Code": { supported: true },
    "PKCE": { supported: true },
    "Hybrid Flow": { supported: true }
  }
}
```

| `SAML_INRESPONSETO_MISMATCH` | Explain SP-initiated vs IdP-initiated flow difference |

Fix in KZero:

```

**Enhanced:**
```
Fix in KZero:
1. Go to your KZero admin panel
   → Navigate to: Clients → [Select Client] → Settings tab

2. Find the "Master SAML Processing URL" field
   📍 Located in the "Access settings" section
   
3. Enter the exact ACS URL: https://vendor.example.com/saml/acs
   ⚠️ Important: The URL must match EXACTLY - including https:// and any trailing slash
   
4. Click "Save" at the bottom of the page

📖 For more details, see KZero docs: docs.kzero.com → Clients → SAML Settings
```

# FINAL PLAN: Beginner-Friendly SSO Troubleshooting for KZero

## What I've Learned

### From Your Admin UI (Browser MCP)
**KZero Dashboard Navigation:**
1. Select tenant
2. Go to Integrations → Applications
3. Choose SAML or OIDC from Custom section
4. For advanced settings: Click "Advanced Console" → Client → search for app

**Key Configuration Sections:**
- **General settings**: Client ID, Name, Description, Always Display in UI
- **Access settings**: Home URL, Valid Redirect URIs, IDP-Initiated SSO URL Name
- **SAML Capabilities**: Name ID format, Force NameID Format, Force POST Binding, Include AuthnStatement
- **Signature & Encryption**: Sign Documents, Sign Assertions
- **Capability Config** (OIDC): Client Authentication, Standard Flow, PKCE

### From Partner Portal Guides
**URL Patterns:**
- IdP Entity ID: `https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>`
- IdP SSO URL: `https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>/protocol/saml`
- SAML Metadata: `https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>/protocol/saml/descriptor`
- OIDC Discovery: `https://ca.auth.kzero.com/realms/<TENANT_NAME>/.well-known/openid-configuration`

**Guide URL Pattern:**
- `https://kzpp.vercel.app/library/admin-guides/sso-configuration/[folder]/[vendor]-sso-configuration`

--- Part 4623 ---
Based on my exploration, I now have a complete picture. Let me summarize the navigation differences:

**Admin UI Navigation (Both SAML and OIDC use the same path):**
1. KZero Dashboard → Select tenant
2. Go to Integrations → Applications
3. In Custom section, choose **SAML** or **OIDC** (this is the protocol choice)
4. Complete the form and click "Add Integration"
5. For advanced settings: Click **Advanced Console** → Select **Client** → search for app

**Key difference:**
- **SAML apps**: Custom → SAML, then SAML-specific tabs (Settings, Keys, Credentials, Advanced, Client scopes)
- **OIDC apps**: Custom → OIDC, then OIDC-specific tabs (Settings, Roles, Client scopes, Advanced, Events)

## Vendor Guide Detection

When the SP's Entity ID/redirect URL contains known vendor patterns, link to the specific guide:

```typescript
const vendorGuidePatterns = {
  "activecampaign.com": "active-campaign-sso-configuration",
  "drata.com": "drata-sso-configuration",
  "shopify.com": "shopify-sso-configuration",
  // ... more patterns
};

const getVendorGuideUrl = (url: string): string | null => {
  for (const [pattern, guide] of Object.entries(vendorGuidePatterns)) {
    if (url.includes(pattern)) {
      return `https://kzpp.vercel.app/library/admin-guides/sso-configuration/[folder]/${guide}`;
    }
  }
  return null;
};
```

### Enhanced Recipe Structure

```typescript
interface RecipeSection {
  title: string;
  owner: Owner;
  bullets: string[];
  kzeroFields?: string[];
  vendorFields?: string[];
  fieldExpectations?: FieldExpectation[];
  
  // NEW FIELDS
  navigationPath?: string;           // "Integrations → Applications → Advanced Console → Client"
  tooltip?: string;                  // Plain-English explanation for tooltips
  vendorGuideUrl?: string;          // Link to vendor-specific guide if detected
  docUrl?: string;                  // Link to KZero docs
  sectionHelp?: {
    what: string;                   // "What this field does"
    why: string;                    // "Why it matters if wrong"
    commonMistakes?: string[];      // Common errors to watch for
  };
}
```

### Tooltip Example Output

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ℹ️ What does this mean?               │
├────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ The ACS URL is the "delivery address"  │
│ for the login confirmation. The vendor  │
│ app won't know you logged in if this   │
│ is wrong.                             │
│                                        │
│ ⚠️ Common mistakes:                    │
│ • Forgetting trailing slash            │
│ • Using http:// instead of https://    │
└────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Goal

Improve the KZero Passwordless SSO Tracer Chrome extension's troubleshooting UX for level 1 technicians. Specifically:
1. Make fix steps beginner-friendly with plain-English explanations
2. Add step-by-step navigation to KZero admin UI
3. Link to vendor-specific SSO guides when SP can be detected
4. Convert documentation links from non-clickable markdown to clickable links
5. Reduce "noise" from successful logins generating unnecessary findings

## Instructions

- Work incrementally, commit after each phase
- Build and test after each change
- KZero supports SAML POST binding, OIDC, and IdP-initiated SSO
- Do NOT mention competitors (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace) anywhere
- Navigation should use Advanced Console only (NOT Integrations/Applications)
- Documentation links must be clickable (not just text URLs)
- Need to address "noise" - successful logins generating findings

## Discoveries

- **KZero Admin UI Structure**: Simplified navigation is `Advanced Console → Clients → [Search App]` (NOT Integrations/Applications)
- **Partner Portal SSO Guides**: Located at `kzpp.vercel.app/library/admin-guides/sso-configuration/[folder]/[vendor]-sso-configuration`
- **Documentation URLs**: `docs.kzero.com/server_admin/index.html` with anchor links
- **Vendor Detection**: 50+ vendor patterns mapped to guide URLs for auto-linking
- **Successful Login "Noise"**: Zoho CRM test showed successful SSO login generated many unnecessary findings - needs investigation/filtering

## Accomplished

### Phase 1: Critical Issues ✅

--- Part 5158 ---
Now I understand the rules better. Looking at the SAML rules, I can see several rules that fire based on conditions that might be present even in a successful login:

1. `SAML_DOCUMENT_SIGNATURE_MISSING` (severity: info) - Line 205-221 - fires if there's no document-level signature, which might be normal
2. `SAML_MISSING_REQUEST` (severity: warning) - Line 30-46 - fires if no AuthnRequest, which is normal for IdP-initiated flows
3. `SAML_IDP_SP_INIT_MISMATCH_CLUE` (severity: info) - Line 323-339 - fires if there's InResponseTo but no AuthnRequest, which can be normal
4. `SAML_WRONG_BINDING_CLUE` (severity: warning) - Line 341-357 - fires if response binding is redirect, might be normal
5. `SAML_RELAYSTATE_UNEXPECTED` (severity: warning) - Line 169-185 - fires if relayState behavior seems unusual

The issue is that these rules don't check if the login was actually successful. They just check for technical conditions that might exist even in successful logins.

To fix the "noise" issue, I need to determine what indicates a successful login. Let me look for how the extension determines success - likely by checking if there's a SAMLResponse with a successful status code, or by checking if the user was authenticated.

1. SAML: A valid SAMLResponse is present with status code 200 (or no error status)
2. OIDC: The token endpoint returns successfully with an access token or ID token

--- Part 5169 ---
Now I need to understand what constitutes a "successful login". Let me look at how the response status is tracked. In SAML, if there's a SAMLResponse with status code 200 and a valid NameID, that's typically success.

Let me look for any existing indication of success in the rules or if there's a way to determine if the login succeeded. The key would be:

For SAML:
- A responseEvent exists
- The status code is 200 (or less than 400)
- There's a NameID present

For OIDC:
- The token endpoint returns successfully
- There's an access token or ID token

The problem is that many of the rules fire even when the login is successful because they just check for technical conditions that might be normal. For example:
- Document signature might not be required
- RelayState behavior might be different for IdP-initiated flows
- Request might be missing for IdP-initiated flows

To fix this, I should add logic to suppress certain "informational" findings when the login appears to have succeeded. Let me create a helper function that determines if a login was successful, and then filter out "info" severity findings when success is detected.

--- Part 5178 ---
Looking at the code, I can see how findings are displayed. The key issue is that all findings are shown regardless of whether the login was successful. The user is saying that successful logins generate "noise" - i.e., informational findings that aren't relevant when the login actually succeeded.

Looking at the rules:
1. `SAML_DOCUMENT_SIGNATURE_MISSING` (severity: info) - Line 205-221
2. `SAML_IDP_SP_INIT_MISMATCH_CLUE` (severity: info) - Line 323-339
3. `SAML_AUTHNREQUEST_SIGN_EXPECTATION_MISMATCH` (severity: info) - Line 395-411
4. `SAML_POLICY_MISMATCH_CLUE` (severity: info) - Line 377-393
5. `OIDC_ACCESS_TOKEN_OPAQUE` (severity: info) - Line 298-314

And warning level findings that might be expected in certain configurations:
- `SAML_MISSING_REQUEST` (severity: warning) - normal for IdP-initiated
- `SAML_RELAYSTATE_UNEXPECTED` (severity: warning)
- `SAML_WRONG_BINDING_CLUE` (severity: warning)
- `SAML_INRESPONSETO_MISSING` (severity: warning) - normal for IdP-initiated
- `SAML_ASSERTION_ENCRYPTED` (severity: warning)

The approach should be:
1. Detect if the login was successful (presence of SAMLResponse with NameID, or OIDC token with access token)
2. When successful, suppress "info" severity findings that don't represent actual problems (e.g., document signature missing when not required)
3. Possibly suppress certain "warning" findings that are normal for IdP-initiated flows

Let me create a solution. The key is to add a function that determines if the login was successful, and then filter out "info" findings when the login succeeded.


I'll add a success detection function to the rules engine and use it to suppress low-severity findings when we have evidence of a successful login.

--- Part 5200 ---
Fixed the noise issue. When login succeeds, these findings are now suppressed:

**Always filtered (info severity):**
- `SAML_DOCUMENT_SIGNATURE_MISSING` - Document signature optional in most configs
- `OIDC_ACCESS_TOKEN_OPAQUE` - Normal for many integrations

**Filtered on successful login (info severity):**
- `SAML_IDP_SP_INIT_MISMATCH_CLUE` - Normal for IdP-initiated flows
- `SAML_AUTHNREQUEST_SIGN_EXPECTATION_MISMATCH` - Normal when SP doesn't sign
- `SAML_POLICY_MISMATCH_CLUE` - Policy differences are informational

**Filtered on IdP-initiated success (warning severity):**
- `SAML_MISSING_REQUEST` - Expected for IdP-initiated
- `SAML_INRESPONSETO_MISSING` - Expected when no AuthnRequest captured

Error and critical findings remain. Load the extension to test with a successful Zoho login - you should see fewer findings now.
